{"id":"holomush-c3ww.30","title":"Fix(holomush-c3ww.26): Add context-cancelled test for Engine.Evaluate","description":"Add test that verifies Engine.Evaluate returns context.Canceled error and zero-value Decision when context is already cancelled.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-11T02:14:04Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-11T02:14:04Z"}

{"id":"holomush-c3ww.26","title":"Engine.Evaluate context-cancelled path returns zero-value Decision with no audit entry","description":"internal/access/policy/engine.go Evaluate() Step 0a (line ~50): when context is cancelled, the function immediately returns types.Decision{} (zero-value) and a wrapped context error. The zero-value Decision has Effect=EffectDefaultDeny (iota 0) and empty PolicyID — it does NOT have PolicyID starting with 'infra:' so Decision.IsInfraFailure() returns false.

Callers in world.Service.checkAccess() branch on evalErr != nil and classify this as ErrAccessEvaluationFailed (correct). However:

1. The returned zero-value Decision is not a valid infra-deny decision — if a caller checked decision.IsInfraFailure() before checking err != nil, they would get a misleading false.
2. No audit entry is written for context-cancelled evaluations, meaning cancellation events are invisible in audit logs.
3. No test covers this path end-to-end: TestEngine_* does not include a context-already-cancelled case.

The engine_contract_test.go covers session errors and cache staleness but not context cancellation.

Suggested test:
  ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
  cancel() // cancel immediately
  decision, err := engine.Evaluate(ctx, validReq)
  require.Error(t, err)
  assert.ErrorIs(t, err, context.Canceled)
  // The returned decision is zero-value — document this contract
  assert.False(t, decision.IsAllowed())

Criticality: 7/10 — in high-load scenarios context cancellation is common; the gap between the returned Decision state and IsInfraFailure() could mislead future callers.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-11T02:08:13Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-11T02:08:13Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-c3ww.27","title":"listCommandsFn circuit breaker comment says 'per-capability' but counts per-command","description":"In internal/plugin/hostfunc/commands.go, the listCommandsFn circuit breaker comment at lines 93-96 says 'After this many per-capability engine errors (not per-command)' but the implementation increments engineErrorCount once per canExecuteCommand() call, which returns a single hadError bool regardless of how many capabilities failed. If a command has 3 capabilities and all 3 fail, engineErrorCount is incremented by 1, not 3. The comment implies the threshold is capability-granular when it is actually command-granular. The behavior is safe (harder to trip than documented) but the comment creates a false mental model of the breaker trigger condition. Suggested fix: update the comment to say 'per-command with any engine error' or change the implementation to pass per-capability error counts back from canExecuteCommand.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-11T02:08:32Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-11T02:08:32Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-c3ww.23","title":"WithRateLimiter option error path not tested through NewDispatcher","description":"internal/command/dispatcher_test.go:1510-1527 explicitly comments that the actual optErr path via WithRateLimiter is not covered: 'We can't easily test this without a mock, but the error path is covered by the optErr field.'

The production code path in dispatcher.go NewDispatcher() applies WithRateLimiter option which calls NewRateLimitMiddleware(rl, d.engine). If NewRateLimitMiddleware returns an error (e.g., nil limiter), it sets d.optErr and NewDispatcher returns that error. This path is exercised in TestNewRateLimitMiddleware (unit) but NOT through NewDispatcher itself with a real WithRateLimiter option call.

Missing test: NewDispatcher(reg, engine, WithRateLimiter(nil)) should return ErrNilRateLimiter and nil dispatcher. This is straightforward to add without mocks — WithRateLimiter(nil) will cause NewRateLimitMiddleware to fail with ErrNilRateLimiter.

Suggested test:
  rl := (*RateLimiter)(nil)  // or simply pass nil
  dispatcher, err := NewDispatcher(reg, validEngine, WithRateLimiter(nil))
  require.Error(t, err)
  assert.ErrorIs(t, err, ErrNilRateLimiter)
  assert.Nil(t, dispatcher)

Without this test a regression in optErr propagation through NewDispatcher would not be caught.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-11T02:07:38Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-11T02:07:38Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-c3ww.25","title":"PlayerMessage suffix-matching against arbitrary codes not guarded by tests","description":"internal/command/errors.go PlayerMessage() uses strings.HasSuffix to match any code ending in '_ACCESS_EVALUATION_FAILED' or '_ACCESS_DENIED'. The test in errors_test.go TestPlayerMessage_SuffixMatching covers the intended entity prefixes (LOCATION, CHARACTER, OBJECT, EXIT).

However, there is no test verifying that an unrelated code that coincidentally ends with these suffixes also triggers the suffix branch. More importantly, there is no test for the edge case where a code ends in '_ACCESS_DENIED' but should NOT match — for example a future code like 'UNAUTHORIZED_ACCESS_DENIED' that could be accidentally caught by the suffix check and return the wrong player message instead of falling through to the default case.

The current test coverage confirms the positive cases but not the boundary of the suffix pattern. This is a low-risk gap since the suffix approach is intentional, but a regression test asserting exact behavior for at least one non-entity-prefix suffix hit and one negative case would make the contract explicit.

Criticality: 3/10 — pattern is intentional by design and tested for the documented cases.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-11T02:08:00Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-11T02:08:00Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-c3ww.24","title":"world.Service entity-specific error codes untested for non-Location service methods","description":"internal/world/service.go checkAccess() is shared by all world service methods and produces entity-scoped error codes (LOCATION_ACCESS_DENIED, CHARACTER_ACCESS_DENIED, EXIT_ACCESS_DENIED, OBJECT_ACCESS_DENIED, SCENE_ACCESS_DENIED) depending on the entityPrefix parameter.

The test in service_test.go TestWorldService_GetLocation covers the Location prefix thoroughly (engine error, infra failure, explicit deny, default deny, oops context). However, the other entity methods (CreateLocation, UpdateLocation, DeleteLocation, GetCharacter, CreateExit, etc.) only test the happy path and basic permission-denied case — none verify that the correct entity-scoped oops error code is produced for each prefix value.

This matters because a copy-paste error in passing the wrong entityPrefix constant to checkAccess() would not be caught (e.g., passing prefixCharacter where prefixLocation is required). The entityPrefix typed string was introduced precisely to prevent this, but no test validates the produced code per method.

Suggested tests for each service method that calls checkAccess:
  - assert oopsErr.Code() == 'CHARACTER_ACCESS_DENIED' for GetCharacter denial
  - assert oopsErr.Code() == 'EXIT_ACCESS_EVALUATION_FAILED' for CreateExit engine error
  - etc.

Criticality: 4/10 — the typed entityPrefix prevents most typos at compile time, reducing risk.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-11T02:07:50Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-11T02:07:50Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-c3ww.39","title":"ReplayWAL partial failure returns error but previously-replayed entries may be re-replayed on next attempt","description":"The WAL replay fix (diff lines 2554-2562) correctly prevents truncation when some entries fail. However, entries that were successfully replayed (written to DB) will be replayed again on the next ReplayWAL call since the WAL is preserved in full. This causes duplicate audit entries in the database.

Location: internal/access/policy/audit/logger.go ReplayWAL() — the 'failures > 0' guard at diff line 2557

The issue: if entries 1-5 succeed and entry 6 fails, the WAL is preserved. On the next ReplayWAL, entries 1-5 are written to the DB again (duplicates).

Fix options:
1. Write successfully replayed entries to a separate 'replayed' file, then on next attempt skip entries already in the replayed file
2. Truncate only the successfully replayed portion of the WAL (rewrite the file with just the failed entries)
3. Accept duplicates if the audit table has idempotent inserts (e.g., UPSERT on a unique key like timestamp+subject+action+resource)

Option 3 is simplest if the audit schema supports it. Otherwise option 2 (rewrite WAL with only failed entries) is the cleanest approach.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-11T02:37:52Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-11T02:37:52Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-c3ww.47","title":"Fix(holomush-c3ww.40): Return zero Decision on attribute resolution error","description":"Change engine.go Step 3 to return types.Decision{} instead of a populated DefaultDeny Decision when attribute resolution fails","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-11T11:46:55Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-11T11:46:55Z"}

{"id":"holomush-c3ww.36","title":"CheckCapability reqErr path missing ErrCapabilityCheckFailed sentinel","description":"In internal/command/access.go, the reqErr path (line ~43-49) wraps only reqErr without joining ErrCapabilityCheckFailed:

    return oops.Code(CodeAccessEvaluationFailed).
        With("command", cmdName).
        With("capability", capability).
        Wrap(reqErr)

But the evalErr path (line ~58-63) and infra failure path (line ~75-80) both include the sentinel:

    Wrap(errors.Join(ErrCapabilityCheckFailed, evalErr))

This means errors.Is(err, ErrCapabilityCheckFailed) will return false for request construction failures, contradicting the docstring: 'Callers can use errors.Is(err, ErrCapabilityCheckFailed) to detect infra failures as distinct from policy denials'.

Fix: change the reqErr path to Wrap(errors.Join(ErrCapabilityCheckFailed, reqErr)) for consistency with the other two branches. Confidence: 85.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-11T02:34:55Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-11T02:34:55Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-c3ww.42","title":"checkAccess NewAccessRequest error branch is untested dead code in world service","description":"The checkAccess helper in internal/world/service.go has a reqErr branch (lines ~14178-14184 in diff) that fires when types.NewAccessRequest() returns an error (empty subject, action, or resource). The branch exists as defensive coding but has zero test coverage and is effectively dead code.

Location: internal/world/service.go, checkAccess() function, reqErr branch

Suggested fix options:
1. Add a comment documenting that this path is unreachable in current usage (preferred — low cost)
2. Add a unit test that directly calls checkAccess via a test helper with an invalid request (harder — requires exported test helper or internal test package)
3. Remove the branch and rely on the panic from typed helpers (not recommended — removes defense in depth)

The dispatcher_test.go does cover the equivalent path for command access checks (TestDispatch_InvalidAccessRequest_ReturnsAccessEvaluationFailed at diff line 5989), so there is precedent for testing this.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-11T02:39:12Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-11T02:39:12Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-c3ww.41","title":"CheckCapability wraps ErrCapabilityCheckFailed with errors.Join losing oops code chain","description":"In CheckCapability (internal/command/access.go, diff lines 5496-5498 and 5514-5516), the infrastructure failure path uses:

    Wrap(errors.Join(ErrCapabilityCheckFailed, evalErr))

and the infra-failure path uses:

    Wrap(errors.Join(ErrCapabilityCheckFailed, errors.New(decision.Reason())))

Using errors.Join creates a multi-error that may not preserve the oops error chain for errutil.LogErrorContext extraction. When the caller logs this error, oops.AsOops() may not find the inner oops metadata (code, context) from evalErr because errors.Join wraps differently than oops.Wrap.

Additionally, on the infra failure path (line 5516), decision.Reason() is converted to a plain errors.New(), losing any structured context from the engine decision. The decision.PolicyID() is in the oops.With context but the reason is duplicated as both a context key AND an error string.

This is a minor issue since the outer oops.Code(CodeAccessEvaluationFailed) with .With() context provides the important debugging info. But for deep error chain inspection, the inner errors may not be extractable.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-11T02:38:17Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-11T02:38:17Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-c3ww.38","title":"Audit log failures in engine.Evaluate are logged but return nil error -- no caller awareness of audit gaps","description":"In engine.go Evaluate(), all four audit.Log() error paths now properly log with slog.WarnContext instead of the previous '_ = err' swallow (good fix). However, the function still returns nil error to callers when audit logging fails. This means callers have no programmatic way to detect or react to audit logging gaps.

Location: internal/access/policy/engine.go lines ~83, ~159, ~196, ~226 (in the diff at lines 2705-2708, 2786-2788, 2807-2809, 2835-2837)

This is by design (the comment says 'don't fail the decision') and is arguably correct for availability -- a failed audit log should not block access decisions. But for a security-critical system, consider:
1. Incrementing a prometheus counter when audit writes fail (the counter already exists as abac_audit_failures_total in logger.go, but the engine-level failures go to slog only).
2. Adding an 'AuditFailed bool' field to Decision so callers can surface this in responses if desired.

Suggestion: Track engine-level audit failures in a metric so ops teams can alert on gaps.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-11T02:37:39Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-11T02:37:39Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-c3ww.52","title":"list_characters action change silently breaks GetCharactersByLocation for non-admin roles","description":"internal/world/service.go: GetCharactersByLocation changed action from \"read\" to \"list_characters\" (line ~14516). The migration equivalence test (migration_equivalence_test.go) marks this as 'EXPECTED DIVERGENCE' — the new engine is supposedly more permissive. But the test only validates admin/builder/player subjects in isolation with a simplified policy. In production, if the seed policies don't have an explicit permit for action=list_characters on location resources, this call silently denies for any non-admin character who previously had read:location:* access. The production seed policies need a list_characters permit policy, and callers see ErrPermissionDenied (CHARACTER_ACCESS_DENIED) with no log-level indication that this is a migration-introduced behavioral change. The divergence is not validated against real seed policies; only the test bootstrap confirms the new engine allows it for test subjects. Fix: add an explicit list_characters permit rule to seed policies and add a test that validates the production seed grants list_characters to player role characters at accessible locations.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-11T12:36:32Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-11T12:36:32Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-c3ww.53","title":"findCharacterByName loses ErrAccessEvaluationFailed context at WorldError boundary","description":"internal/command/handlers/boot.go, findCharacterByName (~line 7003): When GetCharacter returns world.ErrAccessEvaluationFailed, the code increments errorCount and continues (correct), but errorCount is then checked at the end and returns command.WorldError(..., nil) with WORLD_ERROR code. This means:\\n1. The caller (BootHandler) receives WORLD_ERROR, not ErrAccessEvaluationFailed, so errors.Is(err, world.ErrAccessEvaluationFailed) at the call site returns false.\\n2. PlayerMessage cannot distinguish an infrastructure failure from a normal world error — both map to 'Unable to search for player due to a temporary system error.'\\n3. Operators see only a single line from checkAccess, with no indication that the boot command itself encountered the problem.\\nThe same pattern appears in the who.go WhoHandler: it correctly propagates errorCount to the output message but doesn't distinguish infrastructure failures from unexpected errors in the user-visible notice.\\nFix: In findCharacterByName, when errorCount \u003e 0 and the errors included ErrAccessEvaluationFailed, return an oops error with CodeAccessEvaluationFailed rather than CodeWorldError so callers and PlayerMessage can distinguish the two cases.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-11T12:36:46Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-11T12:36:46Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-c3ww.54","title":"who handler missing anomaly detection for all-ErrAccessEvaluationFailed case","description":"internal/command/handlers/who.go, WhoHandler (~line 10416): The anomaly detection check fires when all sessions have permDeniedCount == len(sessions), logging a warning about possible policy misconfiguration. But there is no equivalent check for the infrastructure failure case: if all sessions return ErrAccessEvaluationFailed (engineErrorCount == len(sessions)), the circuit breaker will trip but there is no elevated WarnContext log that says 'all character lookups failed with engine errors — possible engine outage'. The user sees '(Note: N players could not be displayed due to system errors)' but ops teams don't get an alertable log line distinguishing 'broken policy seed' from 'engine outage'.\\nFix: Add a symmetric anomaly check: if engineErrorCount \u003e 0 \u0026\u0026 engineErrorCount == len(sessions), emit a slog.WarnContext with message 'who handler: all sessions failed with engine errors — possible engine outage' including total_sessions and engine_error_count fields.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-11T12:37:10Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-11T12:37:10Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-c3ww.60","title":"TestMigrationEquivalence covers guest subject in map but no test cases exercise it","description":"In internal/access/migration_equivalence_test.go, bootstrapStaticEngine() and bootstrapPolicyEngine() both register 'character:guest-01GHI' with a player/guest role assignment. The comment says 'Guests use player role in static engine' but there is no assertion verifying that: (a) the guest subject maps correctly to the player role in the policy engine, (b) the guest is denied admin/builder operations, or (c) the guest's policy engine decisions match the static engine.

{"id":"holomush-wfza.1","title":"boot handler uses slog.Error instead of slog.ErrorContext, losing request context","description":"internal/command/handlers/boot.go (added in this PR): the access evaluation error is logged with slog.Error instead of slog.ErrorContext. This loses the request context (trace IDs, session metadata) that slog.ErrorContext would propagate.

The dispatcher.go and rate_limit_middleware.go both correctly use slog.ErrorContext, but boot.go uses bare slog.Error.

Additionally, the project standard in CLAUDE.md says to use errutil.LogError for oops errors, but all three call sites (dispatcher.go, boot.go, rate_limit_middleware.go) log directly with slog instead of errutil.LogError. This means oops context fields (error codes, With() attributes) may not be formatted correctly.

Suggested fix in boot.go:
  slog.ErrorContext(ctx, "boot access evaluation failed", "subject", subjectID, "error", evalErr)

And consider using errutil.LogError across all three new Evaluate() error log sites to respect the project standard.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-11T14:04:08Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-11T14:04:08Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-wfza.10","title":"Migration equivalence test lacks boundary condition tests for documented divergences","description":"In internal/access/migration_equivalence_test.go, TestMigrationEquivalence documents 5 cases of known divergence between the static engine and the new policy engine (lines 109, 204, 241-263). These divergences are all described as 'new engine is MORE permissive'. The test asserts the direction (static=deny, policy=allow) but does not test the converse boundary condition — i.e., that the policy engine does NOT grant MORE than intended for subjects that should be denied.

Specifically, the 'player - read other character' and 'player - read location' divergences are documented as acceptable because the static engine uses $here token with LocationResolver. However, there are no tests confirming that:
1. A completely unknown subject (not in any role) is still denied by the policy engine
2. An admin cannot abuse the list_characters decomposition to bypass other access controls

This is low priority since the documented divergences are intentional and separately validated by integration tests in test/integration/access/location_equivalence_test.go, but an explicit comment noting 'integration tests in test/integration/access/location_equivalence_test.go provide full validation of these divergences' would improve maintainability.

File: internal/access/migration_equivalence_test.go","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-11T14:07:59Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-11T14:07:59Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-wfza.2","title":"Evaluate() errors logged with slog directly instead of errutil.LogError, violating project standard","description":"CLAUDE.md states: 'For logging oops errors, use pkg/errutil: errutil.LogError(logger, "operation failed", err)'. The policy engine returns oops-wrapped errors, but all three new Evaluate() error log sites in this PR log directly via slog rather than errutil.LogError:

1. internal/command/dispatcher.go — slog.ErrorContext(ctx, "access evaluation failed", ...)
2. internal/command/handlers/boot.go — slog.Error("boot access evaluation failed", ...)
3. internal/command/rate_limit_middleware.go — slog.WarnContext(ctx, "rate limit bypass check failed", ...)
4. internal/plugin/hostfunc/commands.go — slog.ErrorContext(ctx, "access evaluation failed", ...)

oops errors carry structured context (error codes, With() fields) that errutil.LogError knows how to unpack into structured log fields. Using slog directly may flatten the oops context into a single error string, losing debugging detail.

Suggested fix: replace slog.ErrorContext(ctx, msg, "error", err) with errutil.LogError(logger, msg, err) and pass additional fields as context.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-11T14:04:16Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-11T14:04:16Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-wfza.3","title":"Rate limit bypass failure mode: error logs as Warn but comment says error, inconsistent severity","description":"In internal/command/rate_limit_middleware.go, the Evaluate() error path was changed from slog.ErrorContext to slog.WarnContext with the comment 'Fail-closed on evaluation error: apply rate limiting rather than bypassing'.

However, a policy engine evaluation failure is a genuine infrastructure error — it means the ABAC system is malfunctioning. Logging it at Warn level under-represents the severity and may cause operators to miss that the policy engine is broken. Operators watching only error-level alerts will not see these failures.

The dispatcher.go and boot.go log the same failure type at Error level, creating inconsistency.

Recommendation: keep at Error/ErrorContext level. The fact that the behavior is safe (fail-closed) does not make the underlying engine failure less severe. Rate-limited users will see errors but operators need to know WHY via proper severity.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-11T14:04:26Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-11T14:04:26Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-wfza.21","title":"Session expiry marked as IsInfraFailure causing spurious error logs and failure metrics","description":"In internal/access/policy/engine.go (Evaluate, Step 2), SESSION_INVALID errors produce a decision with PolicyID 'infra:session-invalid'. Since IsInfraFailure() checks strings.HasPrefix(policyID, 'infra:'), this causes SESSION_INVALID to be classified as an infrastructure failure. This propagates to every call site:

- world/service.go checkAccess: logs slog.ErrorContext and calls observability.RecordEngineFailure for each expired-session access attempt
- command/access.go CheckCapability: logs slog.ErrorContext and calls observability.RecordEngineFailure
- command/rate_limit_middleware.go hasBypass: constructs an error from decision.Reason() and passes it to Enforce() which logs slog.WarnContext and records engine failure metric

Session expiry is expected behavior (not an infrastructure failure) and will generate spurious error log entries and holomush_engine_failures_total metric increments for every expired session that attempts to act. This will make the engine failure alert unreliable and flood logs with false-positive errors.

Fix: Either (1) use a different prefix for session invalidity (e.g., 'deny:session-invalid') and reserve 'infra:' for genuine infrastructure errors (store errors, cache staleness, etc.), or (2) add a separate IsSessionInvalid() / IsKnownDeny() predicate so callers can distinguish expected denials from true infrastructure failures without relying on the infra: prefix.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-11T20:13:51Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-11T20:13:51Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-wfza.26","title":"Fix(holomush-wfza.21): change SESSION_INVALID prefix from infra: to deny:","description":"Change policyID from 'infra:session-invalid' to 'deny:session-invalid' in engine.go Evaluate step 2. Update any tests that assert on this prefix.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-11T21:25:20Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-11T21:25:20Z"}

{"id":"holomush-wfza.19","title":"boot.go findCharacterByName: mixed engine+world errors mask ACCESS_EVALUATION_FAILED code","description":"In internal/command/handlers/boot.go, findCharacterByName tracks accessEvalFailedCount and errorCount separately. When all errors are engine failures, it correctly returns CodeAccessEvaluationFailed. But when there is a mix of engine failures and other errors (accessEvalFailedCount \u003e 0 but accessEvalFailedCount \\!= errorCount), it falls through to the WorldError path with CodeWorldError. This loses the signal that engine outages contributed to the failure — callers and PlayerMessage cannot distinguish a mixed-failure scenario from a pure DB failure. The user-visible message is the same ('Unable to search for player due to a temporary system error'), but the error code is wrong, which affects monitoring/alerting based on error codes. Consider returning CodeAccessEvaluationFailed whenever accessEvalFailedCount \u003e 0, regardless of whether other error types also occurred.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-11T20:13:10Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-11T20:13:10Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-wfza.30","title":"Fix(holomush-wfza.19): prioritize engine error code in boot.go mixed failures","description":"In boot.go findCharacterByName, return CodeAccessEvaluationFailed whenever accessEvalFailedCount \u003e 0, regardless of whether other error types also occurred.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-11T21:26:00Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-11T21:26:00Z"}

{"id":"holomush-wfza.24","title":"IsInfraFailure returns true for allow decisions with infra: prefix — callers not tested for this edge case","description":"TestDecision_IsInfraFailure includes a test case 'allow decision cannot be infra failure' that documents: types.NewDecision(EffectAllow, 'allowed', 'infra:should-not-happen').IsInfraFailure() returns true, comment says 'still detects prefix even if semantically wrong'. However, neither checkAccess nor CheckCapability have tests for what happens when the engine returns an allow decision with an infra: prefix. The production code paths only check IsInfraFailure() inside the \\!decision.IsAllowed() branch, so this edge case cannot actually reach the infra-failure handler in production — but that logic is untested. A test documenting this invariant ('allow decisions are never classified as infra failures in checkAccess even if IsInfraFailure() returns true') would prevent future regressions if the code is refactored. File: internal/world/service.go checkAccess(), internal/command/access.go CheckCapability(), internal/access/policy/types/types_test.go.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-11T20:14:26Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-11T20:14:26Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-wfza.23","title":"who handler permDeniedCount anomaly detection path has no dedicated test","description":"who.go adds anomaly detection: when ALL sessions are denied by the policy engine (permDeniedCount == len(sessions)), it logs a warning about possible policy misconfiguration. A future test should verify: given N sessions that all return EffectDeny, the warning log 'who handler: all sessions denied by policy engine — possible policy misconfiguration' is emitted with the correct total_sessions and permission_denied_count fields. File: internal/command/handlers/who.go lines 9855-9861 of the diff.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-11T20:14:17Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-11T20:14:17Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-wfza.25","title":"Migration equivalence test cannot verify list_characters correctness for admin/builder roles","description":"The migration_equivalence_test.go marks the admin and builder list_characters cases as 'EXPECTED DIVERGENCE: list_characters is a new decomposed action not present in static engine'. {"id":"holomush-wfza.37","title":"[errors] Wildcard resource strings bypass typed access helpers in CreateLocation/Exit/Object","description":"In internal/world/service.go, three methods use raw string literals as resource arguments to checkAccess instead of the typed helper functions introduced in this PR:

  - CreateLocation: checkAccess(ctx, subjectID, "write", "location:*", prefixLocation)
  - CreateExit:     checkAccess(ctx, subjectID, "write", "exit:*", prefixExit)
  - CreateObject:   checkAccess(ctx, subjectID, "write", "object:*", prefixObject)

All other call sites use access.LocationResource(id), access.ExitResource(id), etc., which panic on empty input as a defense-in-depth measure. The wildcard variants do not have corresponding helper functions, so the raw strings are the only option currently.

The inconsistency is minor since wildcards cannot be empty, but it creates two patterns for resource construction and means static analysis (or future refactoring) won't catch these three sites uniformly.

Suggested fix: Add access.LocationResourceWildcard(), access.ExitResourceWildcard(), access.ObjectResourceWildcard() constants (or functions) and use them at these three call sites, making the pattern uniform.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-11T21:59:51Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-11T21:59:51Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-wfza.40","title":"[errors] who.go circuit breaker inflates error count with unskipped sessions","description":"In internal/command/handlers/who.go, when the circuit breaker trips (engineErrorCount >= maxEngineErrors), the remaining sessions are added to errorCount without being checked:

  if engineErrorCount >= maxEngineErrors {
      skipped := len(sessions) - i
      errorCount += skipped
      ...
      break
  }

This means sessions that might have been accessible (would have returned ErrPermissionDenied or ErrNotFound, which are non-error cases) are counted as errors. The user sees '(Note: N players could not be displayed due to system errors)' where N is inflated by sessions that were simply not queried.

The code comment explains this is intentional: 'include skipped sessions in errorCount so the user-visible message reflects ALL players that couldn't be displayed'. This is a design choice, but it's potentially misleading because some of the N players may have been legitimately invisible (permission denied) rather than missing due to an error.

Suggested fix: Either:
(a) Track skipped sessions separately and give a more precise message: 'N players could not be displayed: M due to system errors, K skipped due to engine circuit breaker'
(b) Accept the current behavior but clarify the comment that this over-counts intentionally to give users the most conservative estimate.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-11T22:00:00Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-11T22:00:00Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-wfza.39","title":"[code] hasBypass infra failure path embeds structured context into message string instead of oops.With fields","description":"internal/command/rate_limit_middleware.go:96:

    return false, oops.Errorf("infrastructure failure during bypass check: %s", decision.Reason())

The decision reason is interpolated into the error message string rather than being attached as a structured oops context field. The project convention (CLAUDE.md) is to use oops.With() for context so fields are queryable in structured logs.

Preferred pattern:
    return false, oops.
        With("reason", decision.Reason()).
        With("policy_id", decision.PolicyID()).
        Errorf("infrastructure failure during bypass check")

This is consistent with how checkAccess and CheckCapability attach reason/policy_id as With fields.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-11T21:59:59Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-11T21:59:59Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-wfza.38","title":"[code] WithInitialSnapshot exported from policy package despite being test-only","description":"internal/access/policy/cache.go:83 exports WithInitialSnapshot as a public CacheOption with the doc comment: 'This option is intended for testing.'

Exporting test-only helpers from production packages pollutes the public API, can be accidentally used in production code, and is flagged by the new lint:test-helpers CI check as a general concern. The lint check currently only catches policytest imports, not exported test helpers from production packages.

The option is only used in internal/access/migration_equivalence_test.go:534. Options:
1. Unexport it (withInitialSnapshot) — callers in other packages would need a different approach
2. Move to an internal test helper package or the policytest package
3. Accept as-is with a clear //nolint:testhelper annotation if a linter is added for this pattern

Currently the migration equivalence test is in internal/access/ (not internal/access/policy/), so it must use the exported symbol. Moving the test to the policy package would allow unexporting.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-11T21:59:51Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-11T21:59:51Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-wfza.41","title":"WhoHandler circuit breaker and anomaly detection untested","description":"internal/command/handlers/who.go introduces a maxEngineErrors=3 circuit breaker and anomaly detection (all-denied, all-engine-error) warnings via engineErrorCount and permDeniedCount tracking. The who_test.go diff shows only mechanical replacement of AccessControl mock calls with AccessPolicyEngine mock calls — no new tests exercise: (1) circuit breaker tripping after 3 consecutive engine errors, (2) the all-denied anomaly warning when permDeniedCount \u003e= total, (3) the all-engine-error warning branch. These are non-trivial security behaviors that could silently degrade or suppress access enforcement.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-11T22:01:09Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-11T22:01:09Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-wfza.42","title":"listCommandsFn circuit breaker and incomplete field untested","description":"internal/plugin/hostfunc/commands.go introduces a circuit breaker (maxEngineErrors) in listCommandsFn and a new incomplete result field in the returned Lua table. Missing these means the incomplete signaling contract could silently break.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-11T22:01:15Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-11T22:01:15Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-wfza.45","title":"Resolver fail-closed behavior not validated end-to-end at engine level","description":"internal/access/policy/attribute/resolver.go changed resolveEntity, resolveEnvironment, and safeResolve from fail-open (errors swallowed) to fail-closed (errors returned). The engine_test.go diff adds TestEngine_ResolverError_FailsClosed, but does not cover: (1) environment resolver errors causing fail-closed denial (only entity resolver errors are tested), (2) safeResolve panic recovery returning an error that propagates correctly, (3) partial attribute resolution (some attributes resolved, some errored) still causing full denial. The fail-open to fail-closed change is a security-critical behavior shift — broader coverage is warranted.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-11T22:01:35Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-11T22:01:35Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-wfza.43","title":"PartialReplayError and WAL partial-rewrite logic not covered by new tests","description":"internal/access/policy/audit/logger.go introduces PartialReplayError (a new error type) and rewriting the WAL atomically with only the failed entries on partial replay. The logger_test.go diff does not show tests for: (1) PartialReplayError being returned (and not a generic error) when some WAL entries fail, (2) the WAL file containing exactly the failed entries after partial replay, (3) idempotency of replaying from a rewritten partial-failure WAL. Without this, a regression in WAL rewrite behavior (e.g., truncating the WAL or losing failed entries) would go undetected.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-11T22:01:21Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-11T22:01:21Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-wfza.44","title":"RecordEngineAuditFailure metric increment not verified in tests","description":"internal/access/policy/audit/logger.go adds RecordEngineAuditFailure(), called from engine.go on audit log failures. Without a test, the metric counter could be omitted from call sites or broken without detection, leaving operators blind to audit failures.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-11T22:01:27Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-11T22:01:27Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-wfza.54","title":""char:" subject prefix used in production code instead of "character:" after migration","description":"The PR's stated goal is to replace the legacy 'char:' subject prefix with access.SubjectCharacter ('character:') across all call sites. The engine's validateRequest() rejects subjects that do not match the 'type:id' format with a known type prefix — 'char:' is NOT in the known prefix list (prefix.go line 38-48). Any access check that passes a 'char:' subject to the new AccessPolicyEngine will return INVALID_ENTITY_REF and the caller will treat the error as a deny.

The following production files were NOT migrated:
- internal/command/dispatcher.go:135 — 'subject := "char:" + exec.CharacterID().String()'
- internal/command/handlers/boot.go:41 — 'subjectID := "char:" + exec.CharacterID().String()'
- internal/command/handlers/look.go:17 — 'subjectID := "char:" + exec.CharacterID().String()'
- internal/command/handlers/move.go:26 — 'subjectID := "char:" + exec.CharacterID().String()'
- internal/command/handlers/objects.go:42,206 — 'subjectID := "char:" + exec.CharacterID().String()'
- internal/command/handlers/who.go:36 — 'subjectID := "char:" + exec.CharacterID().String()'
- internal/plugin/hostfunc/commands.go:78 — 'subject := "char:" + charID.String()'

Additionally, world/service.go still uses the old AccessControl bool-return interface and propagates 'char:' subjects from callers unchanged.

Fix: replace all 'char:' + id constructions with access.SubjectCharacter + id.String() (i.e. 'character:' + id). Ensure world/service.go's AccessControl interface is updated or the adapter passes the correct prefix.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-11T23:40:40Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-11T23:40:40Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-wfza.58","title":""char:" prefix not migrated to SubjectCharacter in production call sites","description":"The PR summary states it 'Replaces "char:" subject prefix with access.SubjectCharacter ("character:")' but this migration was NOT done in any production call site.

Files still using the old literal "char:" prefix:
- internal/command/dispatcher.go:135 — subject := "char:" + exec.CharacterID().String()
- internal/command/rate_limit_middleware.go:38 — subject := "char:" + exec.CharacterID().String()
- internal/command/handlers/boot.go:41 — subjectID := "char:" + exec.CharacterID().String()
- internal/command/handlers/look.go:17 — subjectID := "char:" + exec.CharacterID().String()
- internal/command/handlers/move.go:26 — subjectID := "char:" + exec.CharacterID().String()
- internal/command/handlers/objects.go:42,206 — subjectID := "char:" + exec.CharacterID().String()
- internal/command/handlers/who.go:36 — subjectID := "char:" + exec.CharacterID().String()
- internal/plugin/hostfunc/commands.go:78 — subject := "char:" + charID.String()

Additionally, internal/access/static.go:106 still handles only the "char" prefix in its switch (case "char", "session":), NOT "character". The new SubjectCharacter = "character:" constant in prefix.go is defined but never used in production code.

The access.go package doc comment on line 7 still documents the old format: subject: "char:01ABC".

The StaticAccessControl and the callers are internally consistent (both still use "char:"), so there is no runtime breakage today. However:
1. The PR description is incorrect/misleading — it claims the migration happened
2. The SubjectCharacter constant is dead/unused in production
3. Any new code following the new convention would produce subjects that StaticAccessControl silently denies (case "character" falls to default: return false)

Fix: Either complete the migration by updating all call sites to use access.SubjectCharacter and updating static.go to handle "character" prefix, OR revert the SubjectCharacter claim from the PR description and leave it for a future PR.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-11T23:41:15Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-11T23:41:15Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-wfza.65","title":"Tests for command handlers and world service use 'char:' prefix — they validate wrong behavior","description":"All handler and world service tests that exercise access control are hardcoded to expect 'char:' as the subject prefix in mock.EXPECT() calls. Affected test files (all mock 'char:' as the subject):
- internal/command/handlers/boot_test.go (all mock calls: lines 67, 126, 190, 254, 323, 331, 339, etc.)
- internal/command/handlers/who_test.go (lines 56, 102, 144, 186, 193, 265, 273, etc.)
- internal/command/handlers/move_test.go (lines 34, 94, 145, 174, 231, 282, etc.)
- internal/command/handlers/look_test.go (lines 32, 48, 64)
- internal/command/handlers/objects_test.go (lines 57, 95, 208, 220, etc.)
- internal/command/handlers/wall_test.go (Grant calls with 'char:' prefix)
- internal/command/handlers/shutdown_test.go (Grant calls with 'char:' prefix)
- internal/world/service_test.go (subjectID := 'char:' + ulid.Make().String() throughout)

Because the mock access control (accesstest.MockAccessControl / worldtest.MockAccessControl) grants/checks against 'char:' and the production code passes 'char:', tests pass. But they are testing a broken state. Once the access control is wired to the real AccessPolicyEngine, these calls would either fail (INVALID_ENTITY_REF) or never match any policy.

Fix: Update all test subjects to use 'character:' prefix (or access.SubjectCharacter constant) to match what the migrated code should pass.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-11T23:41:54Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-11T23:41:54Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-wfza.64","title":"Legacy 'char:' prefix still used in 5 command handlers and dispatcher after AccessPolicyEngine migration","description":"The PR claims to replace 'char:' with 'character:' (access.SubjectCharacter), but 7 production files still emit the old prefix:

- internal/command/handlers/boot.go:41 — subjectID := "char:" + exec.CharacterID().String()
- internal/command/handlers/move.go:26 — same pattern
- internal/command/handlers/who.go:36 — same pattern
- internal/command/handlers/look.go:17 — same pattern
- internal/command/handlers/objects.go:42,206 — same pattern
- internal/command/dispatcher.go:135 — subject := "char:" + exec.CharacterID().String()
- internal/command/rate_limit_middleware.go:38 — same pattern
- internal/plugin/hostfunc/commands.go:78 — same pattern

All corresponding tests (boot_test.go, move_test.go, who_test.go, look_test.go, objects_test.go, dispatcher_test.go) mock the same 'char:' prefix, so they pass. But this means they are testing the legacy behavior that the PR claims to migrate away from.

The new AccessPolicyEngine.validateRequest() accepts 'type:id' format and internal/access.ParseEntityRef() explicitly rejects 'char:' as INVALID_ENTITY_REF (prefix_test.go:108). When the new engine is wired end-to-end, all command capability checks and world service access checks will fail-closed with INVALID_ENTITY_REF instead of evaluating the policy.

The fix: replace all literal 'char:' concatenations with access.SubjectCharacter constant (already defined in internal/access/prefix.go:14 as 'character:'). Update the corresponding test mocks to match.

Severity is critical: this causes every command to fail the access check or return INVALID_ENTITY_REF when the engine migration is active, effectively breaking all player commands.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-11T23:41:43Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-11T23:41:43Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-wfza.61","title":"boot.go and dispatcher.go use old AccessControl.Check() bool interface — not migrated to Evaluate()","description":"internal/command/handlers/boot.go line 52 and internal/command/dispatcher.go line 157 still call the old AccessControl.Check() bool-return interface directly:

  boot.go:52:  allowed := exec.Services().Access().Check(ctx, subjectID, "execute", "admin.boot")
  dispatcher.go:157: if !d.access.Check(ctx, subject, "execute", cap)

These call sites:
1. Use the old bool API, so any infrastructure error (DB down, context cancelled) silently returns false (deny). The bool interface has no way to distinguish 'denied by policy' from 'denied because engine failed'.
2. Use the 'char:' prefix (covered by holomush-wfza.54), so even if migrated to Evaluate(), they would need the prefix fix too.

The PR's migration goal was to move these call sites to AccessPolicyEngine.Evaluate(). These two were missed.

Fix: update the Services().Access() accessor to return AccessPolicyEngine (or an adapter), and migrate these call sites to Evaluate() with proper (Decision, error) handling and error logging.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-11T23:41:29Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-11T23:41:29Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-wfza.55","title":"Audit log errors silently swallowed with bare '_ = err' in engine.go","description":"In internal/access/policy/engine.go, audit log errors are swallowed using '_ = err' in four locations (lines 70-73, 132-134, 162-165, 200-202). The comment says 'Log error but don't fail the decision' but no logging actually occurs — the error is simply discarded.

For denied access attempts (EffectDeny, EffectDefaultDeny), the audit trail is the security record of who was denied what. Silently dropping audit failures means security-relevant events may disappear without any operator visibility. The audit logger itself has a WAL fallback for sync writes and a Prometheus counter for async failures, so at minimum the engine should delegate to errutil.LogError() or slog.Error() when audit.Log() returns an error, so the failure appears in structured logs.

The pattern appears at:
- engine.go:70-73 (system bypass audit)
- engine.go:132-134 (stale cache deny audit)
- engine.go:162-165 (no-applicable-policies deny audit)
- engine.go:200-202 (final decision audit)

Fix: replace '_ = err' with 'slog.Error("audit log failed", "error", err, "subject", req.Subject, "action", req.Action)' or use 'errutil.LogError(logger, "audit log failed", err)'.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-11T23:40:51Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-11T23:40:51Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-wfza.66","title":"engine_contract_test.go missing test case: 'char:01ABC' should return INVALID_ENTITY_REF","description":"internal/access/policy/engine_contract_test.go:TestContract_MalformedSubjectPrefix tests several malformed subject formats but omits the specific legacy prefix case 'char:01ABC'.

{"id":"holomush-wfza.62","title":"ModeMinimal and ModeDenialsOnly have identical implementation in audit logger","description":"In internal/access/policy/audit/logger.go, the shouldLog() method (lines 157-173) has byte-for-byte identical case arms for ModeMinimal and ModeDenialsOnly. Both log only: EffectDeny, EffectDefaultDeny, EffectSystemBypass.

However, doc.go describes them differently:
  - ModeMinimal: 'Logs denials, default denials, and system bypasses (sync)'
  - ModeDenialsOnly: 'Logs all denials and system bypasses (sync)'

The doc comment for ModeMinimal says 'system bypasses + denials' while ModeDenialsOnly says 'denials + default_deny + system_bypass'. Based on naming, ModeMinimal likely should NOT log system bypasses (only failures), while ModeDenialsOnly should include them.

Having two modes with identical behavior is either:
1. A bug where ModeMinimal should exclude EffectSystemBypass (matching the 'minimal' intent)
2. Dead code — one of the modes should be removed

Fix: Determine intended semantics and either differentiate the implementations or remove the duplicate mode.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-11T23:41:30Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-11T23:41:30Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-wfza.67","title":"Cache.Start() is a no-op stub with no test verifying production wiring uses StartWithListener","description":"internal/access/policy/cache.go:199-205: Cache.Start() is declared with a placeholder implementation that logs a warning and returns nil without starting a LISTEN/NOTIFY goroutine. The production wiring in cmd/holomush/ is expected to call StartWithListener instead, but no test verifies this.

The risk: if production wiring calls Start() instead of StartWithListener(), the cache will never be invalidated on policy changes. Cache.IsStale() will eventually return true (after defaultStalenessThreshold=30s), causing fail-closed behavior for all access checks — silently breaking all game operations.

Suggested coverage:
1. A test in cmd/holomush/deps_test.go or an integration test that verifies the Engine is wired to StartWithListener (not Start) when a Listener is available.
2. Alternatively, make Cache.Start() return an error or panic to enforce that only StartWithListener is used until a real PgListener is implemented.

{"id":"holomush-wfza.78","title":"ErrNilAccessControl sentinel retained in errors.go while all call sites are migrated — stale sentinel","description":"## What's Wrong

internal/command/errors.go line 44-45 retains the ErrNilAccessControl sentinel:
```go
// ErrNilAccessControl is returned when creating a dispatcher with nil access control.
ErrNilAccessControl = oops.Errorf("access control cannot be nil")
```

The PR adds ErrNilDispatcherEngine as the replacement (line 7272 in diff) but does not remove ErrNilAccessControl. The dispatcher.go no longer uses it (it returns ErrNilDispatcherEngine instead), so this is a dead exported sentinel.

This is low-severity but leaving stale exported error sentinels in place is confusing: callers who currently use errors.Is(err, ErrNilAccessControl) will silently get false negatives after the migration. Any code that tests for ErrNilAccessControl no longer has a path that produces it.

## Affected File
/Volumes/Code/github.com/holomush/holomush/.claude/worktrees/agent-ae228c80/internal/command/errors.go

## Suggested Fix
Remove the ErrNilAccessControl sentinel (or mark it deprecated if it is intentionally kept for backward compatibility with external callers). Confirm no test asserts errors.Is(err, ErrNilAccessControl).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:19:27Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-12T00:19:27Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-wfza.79","title":"resolver.Resolve now fails closed on ANY provider error — partial resolution errors propagate as evaluation failure","description":"## What's Changed and Why This Needs Attention

The PR changes attribute/resolver.go Resolve() to aggregate all provider errors and return them via errors.Join(). The engine.go at lines 2617-2625 then treats any non-nil error as a fail-closed condition, returning types.Decision{} and the error rather than evaluating policies against partial data.

This is documented as intentional ('fail-closed on provider errors'), but there is a subtle observability gap:

resolveEntity() writes partial results into bag from successful providers BEFORE returning errors from failed providers. The bag is then discarded by the engine (returns Decision{} on error). This means:

1. A single flaky environment-attribute provider causes the ENTIRE evaluation to fail closed, even for subjects whose character/resource providers succeeded.
2. The error from errors.Join aggregates N provider errors into a single message, but the engine only logs the joined error once via errutil.LogErrorContext. Individual provider errors are no longer logged at the resolveEntity level (the old safeResolve logged them directly). Now errors propagate up and only the summary joined error is logged.

The provider-level errors do still carry oops context (namespace, resolve_type, entity_id), so they are not completely lost. But they are no longer individually logged — only the joined error surfaces at the engine level.

## Question for Discussion
Is the intended behavior: one failing non-critical environment provider (e.g., time-of-day attributes) should deny all requests fail-closed? If so, this is correct. If not, the code should distinguish critical providers (whose failures should fail-closed) from non-critical providers (whose failures degrade to partial resolution).

## Affected Files
/Volumes/Code/github.com/holomush/holomush/.claude/worktrees/agent-ae228c80/internal/access/policy/attribute/resolver.go
/Volumes/Code/github.com/holomush/holomush/.claude/worktrees/agent-ae228c80/internal/access/policy/engine.go lines 107-125","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:19:43Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-12T00:19:43Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-wfza.83","title":"S1 defense-in-depth: no test for system subject injection via gRPC/WebSocket context","description":"The engine.go comment for S1 (system bypass defense) explicitly documents that 'External request contexts are never marked as system, so this blocks any attempt to inject the system subject from gRPC, WebSocket, or other external ingress points.' However, TestEngine_SystemBypass_RejectedWithoutSystemContext only tests the engine in isolation with context.Background() (no system marker). There is no integration-level test that simulates an external ingress path (e.g., a request arriving through the dispatcher or command handler with Subject='system' but without WithSystemSubject in context) to verify the full rejection path end-to-end.

The unit test is correct and sufficient for the engine itself. Priority: suggestion (defense-in-depth is already tested at unit level; this is belt-and-suspenders at integration level).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:22:10Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-12T00:22:10Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-wfza.73","title":"resolver.go: Resolve returns partial bags alongside error but engine discards them, creating caller confusion","description":"internal/access/policy/attribute/resolver.go: Resolve() now returns both a non-nil *AttributeBags and a non-nil error when any provider fails (partial results are written to bags before returning). The existing resolver-level tests assert on bags.Subject[\"working.role\"] after require.Error(t, err), which correctly tests the resolver in isolation. However, engine.go immediately discards the partial bags on error (returns types.Decision{}, oops.Wrap(resolveErr)), making the partial data invisible to callers. This creates a mismatch: resolver.Resolve() returns useful partial data, but the engine interface contract (and engine.go comment) treats any resolver error as fail-closed with no useful state. Future callers of resolver.Resolve() may expect the partial data to be usable and write code that accesses bags even on error — which is fine at the resolver level but leads to subtle policy evaluation against incomplete attributes if caller logic changes. Recommendation: Document clearly in resolver.Resolve's godoc that partial bags are returned on error for diagnostics only, not for policy evaluation. Consider returning nil bags on error to enforce the fail-closed contract at the resolver boundary itself, or add a test at the engine level that confirms bags are discarded on resolver error.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:18:47Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-12T00:18:47Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-wfza.80","title":"Cache.Start returns untyped fmt.Errorf — callers cannot errors.Is against it","description":"## What's Wrong

internal/access/policy/cache.go Cache.Start (diff line 2420) now returns:
```go
return fmt.Errorf("Cache.Start is not implemented — use StartWithListener with a concrete Listener")
```

This is a sentinel error that callers may want to check for at boot time to detect misconfiguration. Using fmt.Errorf with no sentinel variable means callers cannot use errors.Is() to distinguish this specific error from other Cache errors. They must string-match, which is fragile.

This is better than the old silent no-op (which returned nil and just logged a warning), but the improvement is incomplete.

## Affected File
/Volumes/Code/github.com/holomush/holomush/.claude/worktrees/agent-ae228c80/internal/access/policy/cache.go

## Suggested Fix

Define a package-level sentinel:
```go
var ErrCacheStartNotImplemented = errors.New("Cache.Start is not implemented — use StartWithListener with a concrete Listener")
```

Then return it directly:
```go
func (pc *Cache) Start(_ context.Context, _ string) error {
    return ErrCacheStartNotImplemented
}
```

Alternatively, use oops.Code("CACHE_START_NOT_IMPLEMENTED") for consistency with the rest of the codebase.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:19:52Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-12T00:19:52Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-wfza.74","title":"who.go and hostfunc/commands.go have duplicated circuit breaker constant (maxEngineErrors=3) with documentation that they \"may diverge\"","description":"internal/command/handlers/who.go and internal/plugin/hostfunc/commands.go both define const maxEngineErrors = 3 with comments saying they are \"intentionally independent\" and \"may diverge\". This is a YAGNI violation: the two circuit breakers currently have identical thresholds and semantics. The comment creates toil (reviewers must remember to check both) while providing no current benefit. If/when the values truly need to differ, the comment is already there — but having two identical magic constants with divergence warnings is premature. Recommendation: Extract to a shared package constant in internal/command or internal/observability, or at minimum name them differently (whoMaxEngineErrors, commandListMaxEngineErrors) so future changes are obviously intentional. If they truly must stay independent, remove the \"may diverge\" speculation and state a concrete reason.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:18:57Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-12T00:18:57Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-wfza.77","title":"policytest.InfraFailureEngine panics on invalid policyID but the contract it enforces is an internal test concern, not a production guard","description":"internal/access/policy/policytest/helpers.go: NewInfraFailureEngine panics if policyID does not start with 'infra:'. This panic is a design concern: (1) it makes the test helper hostile to callers who make a simple mistake — a returned error would be friendlier in test code, and (2) the panic couples the test helper to the internal IsInfraFailure() implementation detail (the 'infra:' prefix check). If IsInfraFailure() is ever changed to use a different discriminator (e.g., a boolean field), NewInfraFailureEngine's panic would fire for valid test invocations. Recommendation: Replace the panic with a t.Fatal or t.Helper call by accepting a testing.TB parameter, or simply return an error. Panicking in test helpers makes test failures harder to diagnose (no test name in panic output). Minor severity — this is test-only code, but CLAUDE.md's error handling conventions prefer explicit errors over panics.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:19:22Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-12T00:19:22Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-wfza.75","title":"rate_limit_middleware.go: nil-receiver guard removed from Enforce() but callers may hold nil *RateLimitMiddleware","description":"In internal/command/rate_limit_middleware.go, the PR removes the nil-receiver guard from Enforce(): the old code was 'if r == nil || r.limiter == nil { return nil }'. The new code removes this and adds a godoc comment 'Callers must not invoke Enforce on a nil receiver.' The dispatcher.go call site is guarded (it checks 'd.rateLimiter \\!= nil' before calling Enforce), so today there is no nil-receiver call path. However: (1) the previous guard was a safety net for any future call site that forgets the check, (2) the WithRateLimiter option now validates the RateLimiter is non-nil before constructing middleware, but this is enforced at construction time only, (3) test code or future code calling Enforce() on a nil receiver will panic instead of returning a safe no-op. The godoc comment is insufficient protection — it relies on all future callers following the contract. The nil guard is cheap and has no downside. Recommendation: Restore the nil-receiver guard in Enforce() or assert.NotNil at the top. The removal is a defensive regression that violates the principle of safe APIs.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:19:09Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-12T00:19:09Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

Assert RecordEngineAuditFailure was called (via promtestutil.ToFloat64 check, as done in TestRecordEngineAuditFailure_IncrementsCounter)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:21:58Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-12T00:21:58Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-wfza.76","title":"PlayerMessage CodeInvalidName silently falls back to generic message — loses informative error text","description":"## What's Wrong

internal/command/errors.go:180-183 — The CodeInvalidName branch in PlayerMessage was changed from returning err.Error() to looking up oopsErr.Context()["message"], but none of the CodeInvalidName producers (validation.go lines 38, 44, 52; alias.go lines 603, 607) set a "message" context key. The context lookup always returns nil, so the fallback "Invalid name." is always returned.

Before the PR, the old code returned err.Error() which produced informative messages like:
- "alias name cannot be empty"
- "alias name exceeds maximum length of 20"
- "alias name must start with a letter and contain only letters, digits, or _!?@#$%^+-"

After the PR, players see only the generic "Invalid name." for all INVALID_NAME errors. The useful validation detail is silently discarded.

## Affected Files
- /Volumes/Code/github.com/holomush/holomush/.claude/worktrees/agent-ae228c80/internal/command/errors.go lines 180-183
- /Volumes/Code/github.com/holomush/holomush/.claude/worktrees/agent-ae228c80/internal/command/validation.go lines 38, 44, 52
- /Volumes/Code/github.com/holomush/holomush/.claude/worktrees/agent-ae228c80/internal/command/alias.go lines 603, 607

## Suggested Fix

Option A — Revert to err.Error() (simplest, preserves existing behavior):
```go
case CodeInvalidName:
    return err.Error()
```

Option B — Add 'message' context to producers and keep the lookup (forward-looking):
In validation.go and alias.go, change each CodeInvalidName error to include With("message", <the message string>) in the context. Then the new PlayerMessage logic works as intended.

The PR comment says 'INVALID_NAME errors contain helpful context in the message itself' — the new code breaks exactly that invariant without updating the producers.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:19:18Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-12T00:19:18Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

The CheckCapability function also rejects empty capability (resource) strings via types.NewAccessRequest, but this path has no test case.

File: internal/command/access_test.go (diff line 6181)

The companion test TestCheckAccess in service_helpers_test.go does test empty action and empty resource cases, so there is an inconsistency in coverage between the two helper functions. TestCheckCapability_InvalidRequest should add a sub-case for capability=''.

Also: the table-driven TestCheckCapability cases do not test context cancellation (engine returns ctx.Canceled), which CheckCapability wraps into CodeAccessEvaluationFailed. This path is covered in TestCheckAccess but not in TestCheckCapability.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:22:19Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-12T00:22:19Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"description":"File: internal/access/policy/cache.go, Cache.Start method

Cache.Start previously silently logged a warning and returned nil. It now returns oops.Code('CACHE_START_NOT_IMPLEMENTED').Errorf(...). A test was added that correctly asserts require.Error.

The risk: any production startup wiring (not in this diff) that calls cache.Start() and treats err != nil as non-fatal will silently continue without a functioning LISTEN/NOTIFY listener. The cache will never receive invalidation notifications, and because IsStale() only trips after the staleness threshold, the system will silently serve stale policies for minutes before the staleness check fires.

The production startup code is not included in this diff, so it cannot be confirmed whether callers properly fail-fast on this error. Given that Cache.Start was previously a no-op returning nil, any caller written against the old contract may log the error and continue.

Action required: confirm that all production callers of Cache.Start either (a) never call it (using only StartWithListener), or (b) treat a non-nil return as fatal. If any caller silently swallows this error, stale policies will be enforced indefinitely."}

{"id":"holomush-eulm.7","title":"Cache.Start now returns an error but all callers that previously ignored the nil return may silently ignore CACHE_START_NOT_IMPLEMENTED","description":"File: internal/access/policy/cache.go, Cache.Start method

Cache.Start previously silently logged a warning and returned nil. It now returns oops.Code('CACHE_START_NOT_IMPLEMENTED').Errorf(...). This is a correct improvement, but it creates a regression risk: any caller that previously called Cache.Start() and checked err == nil (always true before) will now receive a non-nil error and must handle it.

The diff shows Cache.Start signature changed from returning nil to returning an error. If there are any production wiring sites (e.g., cmd/holomush/deps.go or similar startup code) that call cache.Start() and either ignore the error or treat it as non-fatal, the system will silently continue without a functioning LISTEN/NOTIFY listener — meaning the cache will never receive invalidation notifications.

A cache that never gets invalidated means stale policies will be silently served forever. This is particularly dangerous because the engine's cache.IsStale() check returns true only after the staleness threshold elapses — so for the first N minutes (staleness duration) the engine will allow requests against stale policies without any error signal.

The diff does not show the startup wiring code changes (cmd/holomush/deps.go is not in the diff). If Cache.Start is called in production startup code without the error being treated as fatal, this is a critical silent failure.

Verify: search for all callers of .Start( in cmd/ and internal/ that receive the return value of Cache.Start to confirm they fail fast on CACHE_START_NOT_IMPLEMENTED, or confirm that Cache.Start is never called in production (only StartWithListener is used).","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T01:24:33Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-12T01:24:33Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

The production startup code is not included in this diff, so it cannot be confirmed whether callers properly fail-fast on this error. Given that Cache.Start was previously a no-op returning nil, any caller written against the old contract may log the error and continue.
