{"id":"holomush-eulm.10","title":"attribute resolver fail-closed behavior not integration-tested end-to-end","description":"The engine's attribute resolver was changed from fail-open (continuing evaluation with empty bags) to fail-closed (returning an error): internal/access/policy/engine.go:2678-2686. This is a semantic inversion — previously a resolver error produced a deny decision silently; now it propagates as a Go error from Evaluate(). The engine_test.go tests cover the new behavior at the engine unit level, but there is no integration test verifying that a resolver failure at the full-stack level (engine -\u003e world.Service.checkAccess -\u003e world operation) surfaces as ErrAccessEvaluationFailed rather than ErrPermissionDenied at the caller. The integration test in test/integration/access/concurrent_engine_test.go focuses on concurrent access, not resolver failures. A test using a mock resolver that returns an error would confirm fail-closed behavior propagates correctly through the call stack. File: internal/access/policy/engine_test.go (missing resolver-error unit test in new fail-closed mode), test/integration/access/ (missing integration test for resolver failure path).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T01:28:36Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-12T01:28:36Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-eulm.2","title":"audit log failures in engine.Evaluate are downgraded to Warn but callers have no signal that the audit was skipped","description":"File: internal/access/policy/engine.go, function Evaluate (multiple locations)

The engine calls audit.RecordEngineAuditFailure() and logs a Warn, but does NOT return the audit error to the caller. This is documented as intentional ('Log error but don't fail the decision'), but creates a silent audit gap that operators can only detect via the abac_audit_engine_failures_total metric.

The problem is that this is an enforcement-mode design decision that was not explicitly tested for the case where BOTH the primary writer AND the WAL fail (the worst-case path in audit/logger.go). When both fail, audit.Log() now returns an AUDIT_WRITE_FAILED oops error (PR fixed the silent drop), but engine.Evaluate() still swallows it into a slog.Warn + metric. This means:

1. The audit gap is not surfaced to the caller
2. There is no way for callers (CheckCapability, checkAccess) to distinguish 'decision made, audit failed' from 'decision made, audit succeeded'
3. An operator running in compliance-required mode cannot detect at the RPC/command level that audit writes are silently failing

There are 4 call sites in engine.Evaluate where auditErr is logged+dropped:
- system bypass path (line ~83)
- cache stale path (line ~159)
- no applicable policies path (line ~197)  
- final decision path (line ~228)

Suggested fix: The engine should accept an audit mode flag (e.g., StrictAudit bool in EngineConfig) that causes audit failures to be returned as an error (possibly wrapped with a 'decision was X but audit failed' decision). For the current non-strict mode the behavior is correct, but strict mode should exist for compliance deployments. Alternatively, the engine could attach audit failure info to the returned Decision so callers can inspect it without breaking the error contract.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T01:23:28Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-12T01:23:28Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-eulm.11","title":"dispatcher: concurrent Dispatch calls with engine failure not tested for metric consistency","description":"The dispatcher now records StatusEngineFailure metrics on access evaluation failures (internal/command/dispatcher.go:6474-6477). The existing tests verify the metric is incremented for a single call (TestDispatch_EngineError_ReturnsAccessEvaluationFailed), but there is no test for concurrent dispatch calls to the same command with engine failures — the prometheus counter is a package-level var and concurrent increments could theoretically race. The test_coverage for this is low-priority since Prometheus counters are goroutine-safe, but a t.Parallel() test that dispatches concurrently with an error engine would confirm no data races under the race detector. {"id":"holomush-eulm.4","title":"resolver.go safeResolve panic handler sets retErr after defer but named return is not guaranteed to override a successful return value","description":"File: internal/access/policy/attribute/resolver.go, functions safeResolve and safeResolveEnvironment

Both functions use named return values (attrs map[string]any, retErr error) with a deferred panic recovery that sets retErr. This pattern is correct Go: the defer can modify named return values and they will be returned.

However, there is a subtle issue in safeResolve: if a panic occurs AFTER attrs has been assigned (e.g., the provider panics during attribute assignment after returning), the function will return (attrs, retErr) — i.e., both non-nil attrs AND a non-nil retErr. The caller (resolveEntity) does:

  attrs, err := r.safeResolve(...)
  if err != nil {
      errs = append(errs, err)
      continue  // does NOT merge attrs into bag
  }

This is correct — partial attrs from a panicking provider are discarded, which is the right fail-closed behavior.

BUT: the panic recovery in safeResolve does NOT set attrs = nil before setting retErr. If a provider returns valid attrs and then panics in some post-return cleanup (impossible in Go — panics during return don't happen), or if the defer runs before the return statement sets attrs... actually in Go this cannot happen.

Wait — the actual issue is: the panic recovery sets retErr but does NOT zero out attrs. If attrs was assigned before the panic, the caller gets (non-nil-attrs, non-nil-err). The caller correctly discards attrs when err != nil, so this is safe. But it's worth documenting that callers MUST check err before using attrs, which is already the contract in the doc comment ('Callers MUST NOT use partial bags for policy evaluation').

Real finding: safeResolveEnvironment uses 'attrs, err = provider.Resolve(ctx)' (assigning to named return attrs) inside the function body, but the deferred recovery only sets retErr. If the provider panics after partially modifying a slice/map that attrs points to, the panic handler sets retErr but attrs may be a partially-mutated map. Since the caller discards attrs on err != nil this is safe, but the recovery should also nil out attrs for defense in depth:

  if recovered := recover(); recovered != nil {
      retErr = oops.Errorf(...)
      attrs = nil  // <-- missing: defense in depth
  }

This is a hardening suggestion, not a correctness bug, since callers already check err.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T01:24:02Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-12T01:24:02Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-eulm.5","title":"who handler: engine outage anomaly detection is unreachable when sessions \u003e maxEngineErrors","description":"In internal/command/handlers/who.go, the anomaly detection check at line ~97 (engineErrorCount \u003e 0 \u0026\u0026 engineErrorCount == len(sessions)) is unreachable when len(sessions) \u003e maxEngineErrors (3). The circuit breaker breaks the for loop after 3 engine errors, so engineErrorCount maxes out at 3, but len(sessions) is typically much larger. This means the 'all sessions failed with engine errors' warning never fires for realistic session counts. The symmetric permDeniedCount check IS correct because permission denied does not trigger the circuit breaker. Suggested fix: compare engineErrorCount+skippedCount \u003e= len(sessions) instead, or remove the check and rely on the circuit breaker's own logging.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T01:24:15Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-12T01:24:15Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-eulm.6","title":"RateLimitMiddleware.Enforce silently drops bypass evaluation error without propagating it to the span/trace","description":"File: internal/command/rate_limit_middleware.go, Enforce method

When hasBypass returns an error, Enforce correctly logs it at Error level and records the metric, then falls through to apply rate limiting (fail-closed, documented and justified). However, the OTel span is not updated — the span.SetStatus() and span.SetAttributes() calls that exist for rate-limit decisions are not called for the bypass-evaluation-failed path.

In dispatcher.go, when Enforce returns an error, metrics.SetStatus(StatusRateLimited) is set. But when Enforce applies rate limiting due to a bypass check failure (not an actual rate limit hit), it falls through to the rate limiting code which will call span.SetAttributes and return ErrRateLimited. From the trace perspective, this looks like a legitimate rate-limit hit, not an engine failure.

This means distributed traces will misattribute engine failures as rate-limit events, making incident debugging harder.

Suggested fix: When the bypass check fails (err != nil from hasBypass), Enforce could set a span attribute like span.SetAttributes(attribute.Bool('rate_limit_bypass_check_failed', true)) before falling through to the rate limiting logic. This doesn't change behavior but makes the trace accurate.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T01:24:16Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-12T01:24:16Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-eulm.1","title":"canExecuteCommand early-returns on first error, inflating circuit breaker count by 1 per command","description":"File: internal/plugin/hostfunc/commands.go, function canExecuteCommand

When a command has multiple capabilities, the function returns (false, true) on the first error without checking remaining capabilities. This is fine for fail-closed semantics, BUT in the outer loop in listCommandsFn, each call to canExecuteCommand counts as exactly 1 against engineErrorCount regardless of how many capabilities the command has. The circuit-breaker comment says 'Each command contributes at most 1 to the count regardless of how many capabilities it has', which is correct — but the early-return means that when the engine errors on the first capability of a multi-cap command, subsequent capabilities for that same command are never tried. This is intentional fail-closed behavior and is documented, so this is fine from a security standpoint.

However there is a subtle mismatch: canExecuteCommand returns early on a request-construction error (empty subject/resource from types.NewAccessRequest) before calling the engine. This means engineErrorCount can increment for a local validation error, not an actual engine failure. The metric label used is 'command_capability_check' in both cases, which makes it impossible to distinguish request-build errors from engine evaluation errors in metrics.

Suggested fix: use separate metric labels, e.g. 'command_capability_check_request_error' vs 'command_capability_check_engine_error', so operators can distinguish the two failure modes.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T01:23:09Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-12T01:23:09Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-eulm.15","title":"Fix(holomush-eulm.1): differentiate metric labels","description":"Differentiate command_capability_request_error vs command_capability_engine_error","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T01:41:51Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-12T01:41:51Z"}

{"id":"holomush-eulm.8","title":"who handler: ErrAccessEvaluationFailed path not tested when world.Service.checkAccess returns it","description":"The who handler's new ErrAccessEvaluationFailed branch (internal/command/handlers/who.go:11203) is tested in TestWhoHandler_AccessEvaluationFailedCountsAsError, but that test uses a mock engine that returns an error directly — which is wrapped by world.Service.checkAccess into ErrAccessEvaluationFailed. What is not tested is whether the who handler correctly handles the case where checkAccess returns ErrAccessEvaluationFailed due to an *infrastructure failure decision* (IsInfraFailure()==true) rather than a raw engine error. The two paths through checkAccess (engine error vs infra failure decision) both produce ErrAccessEvaluationFailed-wrapped errors, but there is no test confirming the who handler shows the user warning for infra-failure-sourced ErrAccessEvaluationFailed errors (where the engine returns nil error but a deny Decision with infra: PolicyID prefix). This matters because the distinction between a deny decision flowing through world.checkAccess and producing ErrAccessEvaluationFailed vs ErrPermissionDenied is subtle and easy to regress. A test verifying who shows the system error notice for infra-failure decisions (not just raw engine errors) would catch regressions in the checkAccess infra-failure branch at the integration level. File: internal/command/handlers/who_test.go (missing test) and internal/world/service.go:15350 (infra-failure branch in checkAccess).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T01:28:13Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-12T01:28:13Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-eulm.3","title":"who.go circuit breaker logs the trip warning inside the loop but after skippedCount is already set, causing log/count mismatch on first trip","description":"File: internal/command/handlers/who.go, WhoHandler function

The circuit-breaker logic checks engineErrorCount >= maxEngineErrors at the top of the loop. On the iteration where it trips, it sets skippedCount = len(sessions) - i (correct), logs the warning, then breaks. This is correct.

However, the warning log fires inside the session loop on EVERY iteration after the circuit trips — but wait, it breaks immediately, so it only fires once. This is actually correct behavior.

A more subtle issue: the circuit breaker fires at the START of an iteration when engineErrorCount >= maxEngineErrors (i.e., AFTER 3 failures). This means the 4th session is never processed (skipped), but the log says 'skipped_sessions = len(sessions) - i'. On the 4th iteration (i=3), skippedCount = len(sessions) - 3, which includes the current session. So the session at index i=3 is correctly counted as skipped. This is correct.

Minor: the warning log says 'aborting after engine failures' but the metric for skipped sessions is only surfaced in user-facing output (writeOutputf), not in a Prometheus metric. Operators watching metrics would see the engineAuditFailures counter but not the skipped session count. Consider adding observability.RecordCircuitBreakerTrip() or at minimum a metric for sessions skipped by the circuit breaker to make this alertable.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T01:23:40Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-12T01:23:40Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-eulm.9","title":"session-invalid reclassification (deny: prefix) not tested at call-site boundary","description":"The ENGINE changed SESSION_INVALID decisions from 'infra:session-invalid' (IsInfraFailure()==true) to 'deny:session-invalid' (IsInfraFailure()==false). This semantic change is tested at the types level (TestDecision_IsInfraFailure) and engine level (TestEngine_SessionInvalid), but there is no call-site test verifying the downstream effect: specifically, that a session-invalid denial is treated as ErrPermissionDenied (silent skip) rather than ErrAccessEvaluationFailed (shown as system error to users) at the who handler and world service levels. If the reclassification were accidentally reverted or the IsInfraFailure() check in checkAccess were modified, session expiry would cause spurious 'system error' notices to users. {"id":"holomush-eulm.19","title":"Session-resolution deny decisions skip audit logging in engine.go","description":"File: internal/access/policy/engine.go, Step 2 (session resolution error path)

When ResolveSession returns an error (both SESSION_INVALID and session store errors), the engine correctly creates a deny decision and now records metrics (RecordEvaluationMetrics added in this PR), but returns at line 669 without ever calling e.audit.Log. Every other decision path in Evaluate — system bypass, staleness, no-candidates, and full policy evaluation — records an audit entry. Session-resolution denials are the only path that produces no audit record.

Impact: any access denial that happens because a session is expired or the session store is unavailable leaves zero audit trail. For a compliance- or security-sensitive system this is a meaningful gap: an attacker replaying an expired session token would be denied but the denial would not appear in audit logs or WAL replays.

The PR added RecordEvaluationMetrics to this path (good), but did not add the matching e.audit.Log call.

Suggested fix — after decision.Validate(), before the return, add:
  entry := audit.Entry{
      Subject:    req.Subject,
      Action:     req.Action,
      Resource:   req.Resource,
      Effect:     decision.Effect(),
      PolicyID:   decision.PolicyID(),
      DurationUS: time.Since(start).Microseconds(),
      Timestamp:  time.Now(),
  }
  if auditErr := e.audit.Log(ctx, entry); auditErr != nil {
      slog.WarnContext(ctx, "audit log failed", "error", auditErr)
      audit.RecordEngineAuditFailure()
  }","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T01:52:14Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-12T01:52:14Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-eulm.22","title":"[code] Unreachable switch case in WhoHandler output display","description":"internal/command/handlers/who.go: The final switch case 'case skippedCount \u003e 0' in the error output block is unreachable dead code. skippedCount is only set when the circuit breaker trips (engineErrorCount \u003e= maxEngineErrors). The circuit breaker only trips after engine errors, each of which also increments errorCount. Therefore, if skippedCount \u003e 0 then errorCount \u003e= 3 \u003e 0, so the outer case 'errorCount \u003e 0 \u0026\u0026 skippedCount \u003e 0' always fires first. The isolated 'case skippedCount \u003e 0' branch (which assumes errorCount == 0) can never execute. This will confuse future maintainers. {"id":"holomush-eulm.20","title":"GetCharactersByLocation uses prefixLocation, producing misleading LOCATION_ACCESS_* error codes","description":"File: internal/world/service.go, GetCharactersByLocation method

GetCharactersByLocation calls checkAccess with prefixLocation:
  s.checkAccess(ctx, subjectID, "list_characters", resource, prefixLocation)

This means evaluation errors produce LOCATION_ACCESS_EVALUATION_FAILED and denials produce LOCATION_ACCESS_DENIED. However, this is a character-query operation — the prefix should semantically be prefixCharacter or a dedicated prefixCharacterQuery to produce CHARACTER_ACCESS_EVALUATION_FAILED / CHARACTER_ACCESS_DENIED codes.

Practical impact:
- Operator metrics/logs for character listing failures are lumped into location counters
- The operation-specific error code (LOCATION_*) does not match the entity being queried (characters)
- The metric key produced is LOCATION_access_check, not CHARACTER_access_check

Both LOCATION_* codes are present in entityAccessEvalFailedCodes and entityAccessDeniedCodes in command/errors.go, so PlayerMessage handling is correct. This is a correctness/observability issue, not a security or silent-failure issue.

Suggested fix: use prefixCharacter, or introduce a new prefixCharacterQuery constant. Update entityAccessEvalFailedCodes and entityAccessDeniedCodes if the new code differs from CHARACTER_ACCESS_*.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T01:52:25Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-12T01:52:25Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

CheckCapability uses lowercase: 'boot_access_check', 'who_access_check'. The Prometheus 'operation' label cardinality is also relatively high (one per entity-type/command). {"id":"holomush-sdno.1","title":"deny:session-invalid policy ID bypasses IsInfraFailure() detection","description":"In internal/access/policy/engine.go (step 2 session resolution), when the session resolver returns a SESSION_INVALID error, the policy ID is set to "deny:session-invalid" (changed from the previous "infra:session-invalid").

IsInfraFailure() in internal/access/policy/types/types.go checks strings.HasPrefix(d.policyID, "infra:"). With the new "deny:" prefix, IsInfraFailure() returns false for session-invalid denials.

This causes callers (world/service.go checkAccess, command/access.go CheckCapability) to classify a session-invalid decision as a policy denial rather than an infrastructure failure. The callers use IsInfraFailure() to distinguish transient errors (which should surface as evaluation failures) from hard policy denials. With this change:
- world.checkAccess will wrap a session-invalid denial with ErrPermissionDenied instead of ErrAccessEvaluationFailed
- command.CheckCapability will return CodePermissionDenied instead of CodeAccessEvaluationFailed
- The who handler's engineErrorCount will not be incremented for session-invalid failures

The debug-level log also silently swallows the session ID into DebugContext, meaning that invalid sessions during access checks will not generate ERROR-level entries and will not trigger the anomaly-detection warning in who.go.

Fix: restore the "infra:session-invalid" prefix, or add "deny:" to the HasPrefix check in IsInfraFailure(). The PR comment says the change was intentional but the comment only says 'deny:session-invalid' is used — there is no corresponding update to IsInfraFailure() and no test for this combination.

Relevant lines:
- engine.go: decision = types.NewDecision(types.EffectDefaultDeny, "session invalid", "deny:session-invalid")
- types.go IsInfraFailure(): return strings.HasPrefix(d.policyID, "infra:")","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T23:28:39Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-12T23:28:39Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-sdno.3","title":"ModeMinimal silently drops system-bypass audit events after behavioral change","description":"In internal/access/policy/audit/logger.go, the ModeMinimal case was changed from logging {deny, default_deny, system_bypass} to logging only {deny, default_deny}. System bypass events are security-relevant: they record that the 'system' subject was used (with the new S1 enforcement in engine.go, this is now also a valid trust boundary event).

The PR updates the comment to say 'minimal mode' does not log system bypasses, but does not document why system bypass events should be silently dropped in minimal mode — which is the mode most likely used in production to reduce audit noise.

The new S1 guard in engine.go correctly rejects system subject usage without system context, but a legitimate system-context bypass still produces a decision with EffectSystemBypass that ModeMinimal will now silently discard. Operators running in ModeMinimal will have no audit trail of successful system bypass evaluations.

For access control systems, omitting system-bypass from the minimal audit trail is a policy decision that should be made explicitly, not as a side effect of updating a comment. If this is intentional, a test should verify that ModeMinimal drops system bypass entries, and the doc should explain the rationale.

Relevant location: internal/access/policy/audit/logger.go shouldLog(), ModeMinimal case, line ~1409 in diff. Previously logged EffectSystemBypass, now does not.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T23:29:04Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-12T23:29:04Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-sdno.13","title":"world/postgres/helpers: TxFromContext exported unnecessarily — YAGNI violation","description":"internal/world/postgres/helpers.go: TxFromContext is exported (capital T) and used only in transactor_test.go (same package tests via package postgres). Per CLAUDE.md YAGNI principle, functions should not be exported without a clear cross-package consumer. The only caller is the integration test within the same package, which could use an unexported helper or access the context key directly. Exporting TxFromContext creates a public API surface that exposes the internal pgx.Tx context key contract to external packages, which is undesirable. Fix: make it unexported (txFromContext) or move the test helper inline.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T23:33:39Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-12T23:33:39Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-sdno.5","title":"oopsErr.Code() == "SESSION_INVALID" compares any to string — fragile type assertion","description":"In internal/access/policy/engine.go, the session resolution error branch compares oopsErr.Code() == "SESSION_INVALID" where oopsErr.Code() returns type 'any' (the oops library Code() method returns interface{}). This comparison works today because Go allows == between 'any' and a string literal when the underlying value is a string, but it is fragile:

1. If a code is set as a non-string type (e.g., a typed constant), the comparison silently falls to the else branch, classifying a session-invalid as a store error.
2. The CLAUDE.md project convention for errutil testing is errutil.AssertErrorCode(t, err, code) which handles this correctly, but the engine uses raw comparison.

The project convention shown in CLAUDE.md is:
  errutil.AssertErrorCode(t, err, "EXPECTED_CODE")

The engine should either use a typed constant for the code or use the oops utility that does the conversion:
  if ok && fmt.Sprint(oopsErr.Code()) == "SESSION_INVALID"

Relevant location: internal/access/policy/engine.go, the conditional:
  if ok && oopsErr.Code() == "SESSION_INVALID"","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T23:29:27Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-12T23:29:27Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-sdno.23","title":"Fix(holomush-sdno.2): return error on audit channel full","description":"In Logger.Log(), return an error when async channel is full instead of nil. This lets engine callers increment audit failure metrics.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-13T00:05:08Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-13T00:05:08Z"}

{"id":"holomush-sdno.8","title":"world/service: ExamineLocation/Object/Character fetch entity before authorization check — TOCTOU and information disclosure","description":"internal/world/service.go ExamineLocation, ExamineObject, ExamineCharacter: All three Examine methods fetch the entity from the repository BEFORE calling checkAccess. The PR adds a comment saying this is intentional ('fetch first lets us provide better error messages'), but this pattern creates two issues: (1) an unauthorized caller can distinguish 'not found' vs 'access denied' (confirmed information disclosure), and (2) the fetch creates an unnecessary DB round-trip that is discarded on denial. The PR's own comment acknowledges the information disclosure. While the comment argues this is acceptable in a MUSH context, the prior code did NOT have this pre-fetch pattern — checkAccess ran first. This is a new regression introduced in this PR. Fix: move the authorization check before the entity fetch, matching the pattern used by all other service methods (GetLocation, GetCharacter, etc.), OR if intentional, add a security note in the ABAC spec/design doc justifying the deviation.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T23:32:47Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-12T23:32:47Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-sdno.6","title":"observability: package-level Prometheus counters risk double-registration panic in tests","description":"internal/observability/server.go: The three new counters (engineFailures, circuitBreakerTrips, circuitBreakerSkipped) are declared as package-level prometheus.NewCounterVec vars AND are also passed to reg.MustRegister() inside NewMetrics. If NewMetrics is called more than once in the same process sharing prometheus.DefaultRegisterer (common in parallel test binaries), MustRegister panics with 'already registered'. Tests in server_test.go that call NewServer() AND the RecordEngineFailure helpers in the same test binary are at risk. Fix: use prometheus.MustRegister only once (either at package init OR in NewMetrics, not both), or use prometheus.NewRegistry() per test.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T23:32:28Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-12T23:32:28Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-sdno.4","title":"Attribute resolution failure skips audit log — access denial goes unrecorded","description":"In internal/access/policy/engine.go, Step 3 (eager attribute resolution), when resolver.Resolve() returns an error the engine immediately returns an empty types.Decision{} with no audit log entry. Every other denial path in Evaluate() records an audit.Entry before returning:
- System bypass: audit.Log called
- Session invalid/store error: audit.Log called (new in this PR)
- Policy cache stale: audit.Log called
- No applicable policies: audit.Log called
- Full evaluation: audit.Log called

Only the attribute resolution failure path lacks an audit entry. This means attribute-provider failures produce silent access denials — they are logged to slog (errutil.LogErrorContext) but not written to the audit trail. Operators reviewing the audit log will see gaps for any requests that fail due to provider errors, and cannot correlate access denials with infrastructure failures.

Fix: emit an audit.Entry with EffectDefaultDeny and a policyID of "infra:attribute-resolution-failed" before returning from the resolve error path, consistent with the session-resolution error pattern introduced in this same PR.

Relevant location: internal/access/policy/engine.go, lines after 'bags, resolveErr := e.resolver.Resolve(ctx, req)' where resolveErr != nil branch returns without audit.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T23:29:16Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-12T23:29:16Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-sdno.2","title":"Async audit channel drop returns nil — engine callers cannot detect audit loss","description":"In internal/access/policy/audit/logger.go Logger.Log(), when the async channel is full the code adds a slog.Warn but still returns nil (no error). The engine callers in engine.go all check the return value of audit.Log and call audit.RecordEngineAuditFailure() on non-nil errors. Because this path returns nil, the RecordEngineAuditFailure metric is NOT incremented for async drops.

As a result:
- The abac_audit_engine_failures_total metric does NOT reflect async channel full events
- Ops teams cannot alert on audit gaps caused by a full async channel
- Only the channelFullCounter (abac_audit_channel_full_total) increments, which is a different metric

The sync path (ModeMinimal/ModeDenialsOnly) correctly propagates errors and lets the engine record failures. The async path (ModeAll for permit effects) silently drops without engine-level tracking.

Fix: return an error from Log() when the channel is full (the async select default case), or at minimum ensure RecordEngineAuditFailure() is called from within Log() itself for channel-full drops so that the metric stays accurate regardless of return value.

Relevant location: internal/access/policy/audit/logger.go, the 'default: // Channel full' case in Log(). Returns nil after slog.Warn.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T23:28:52Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-12T23:28:52Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-sdno.15","title":"resolver: HasNamespace guard for duplicate RegisterProvider has no test","description":"internal/access/policy/attribute/resolver.go changed how duplicate provider registration is handled. Previously it checked for 'already registered' in an error string (fragile). The PR replaces this with r.registry.HasNamespace(namespace) before calling Register().

There is no test in resolver_test.go that verifies registering the same provider namespace twice is idempotent. The test file (line 1263 in diff) updates only the error-on-failure assertions; no test verifies the skip-if-already-registered path for RegisterProvider or RegisterEnvironmentProvider.

A test should:
1. Register a provider once → succeed
2. Register a provider with the same namespace again → still succeed (no error)
3. Verify the provider is only called once (not duplicated) during Resolve

Without this, a regression where HasNamespace is removed would silently break, causing double-registration errors that break engine startup.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T23:39:27Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-12T23:39:27Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-sdno.11","title":"command/access_test.go: TestRecordEngineFailure_IncrementsCounter tests shared package-level counter — test isolation violation","description":"internal/command/access_test.go (inferred from logger_test.go pattern): Tests that call RecordEngineFailure() and then assert the counter value via prometheus/testutil.ToFloat64 are reading from the shared package-level counter. If other tests in the same binary also call RecordEngineFailure(), the counter value will be higher than expected. The test should either use a dedicated prometheus.Registry per test case (passing it explicitly), or use testutil.CollectAndCount with a filtered registry. This is a test pollution issue that can cause flaky test failures in parallel test runs.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T23:33:14Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-12T23:33:14Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-sdno.17","title":"who handler: anomaly detection for all-perm-denied not tested for mixed sessions","description":"internal/command/handlers/who.go adds anomaly detection: if permDeniedCount > 0 && permDeniedCount == len(sessions), it logs a warning about possible policy misconfiguration (line 10671-10677 in diff).

A test covering this path is present at line 11413 ('assert.Contains(t, logOutput, "all sessions denied by policy engine")').

However, the test does not verify that this warning is NOT logged when only SOME sessions are denied (e.g., 1 of 2 denied). A regression that changed '==' to '>=' in the condition would log a spurious misconfiguration warning on every partial-deny scenario, alarming operators.

Add a negative test: with 2 sessions where 1 is denied and 1 is allowed, verify that 'all sessions denied by policy engine' does NOT appear in the log output.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T23:39:48Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-12T23:39:48Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-sdno.18","title":"listCommandsFn: circuit breaker not tested for no-capability commands during trip","description":"internal/plugin/hostfunc/commands.go documents a key invariant (line 13366-13368 in diff): 'commands with no capabilities are ALWAYS included, even when the circuit breaker has tripped.'

The existing TestListCommands_CircuitBreakerTripsAfterThreeErrors test uses a registry with only capability-gated commands, so it cannot verify that no-capability commands survive a circuit breaker trip.

A regression where the 'continue' for circuitTripped (line 13386-13387) moved to before the len(caps)==0 check would silently hide all commands including no-capability ones when the circuit breaker fires. This would make the game feel completely broken.

Add a test with a mixed registry (some commands with capabilities, some without) and an error engine that trips the circuit breaker. Verify that no-capability commands still appear in the result while capability-gated commands are hidden.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T23:39:58Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-12T23:39:58Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-sdno.19","title":"checkAccess: metric key format not independently tested","description":"internal/world/service.go builds the metric key as: strings.ToLower(string(prefix)) + '_access_check' (e.g., 'location_access_check', 'exit_access_check'). This key is passed to observability.RecordEngineFailure().

The service_helpers_test.go tests verify checkAccess error codes and error types for all five prefixes, but no test verifies that RecordEngineFailure is actually called with the correct metric key for each prefix.

The CheckCapability tests in access_test.go do verify metric increments (lines 5706-5709 in diff), but checkAccess in world/service.go uses a different key format. A regression that changed the key format (e.g., removing the prefix or using the wrong separator) would silently produce metrics under the wrong label, breaking Prometheus alert rules.

Add Prometheus testutil assertions to at least one checkAccess test case (engine error path) per entity prefix, similar to how TestCheckCapability verifies observability.EngineFailureCounter().","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T23:40:08Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-12T23:40:08Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-sdno.16","title":"world service_test: ErrAccessEvaluationFailed not tested for most service methods","description":"The PR adds deep engine-error and infra-failure tests for GetLocation (see service_test.go additions) and some Examine* methods. However, the following service methods have permission-denied tests but NO engine-error or infra-failure tests:

- GetExit (denied test present, eval-failed test absent)
- CreateExit (denied test present, eval-failed test absent)
- UpdateExit (denied test present, eval-failed test absent)
- DeleteExit (denied test present, eval-failed test absent)
- GetExitsByLocation (denied test present, eval-failed test absent)
- CreateObject (denied test present, eval-failed test absent)
- UpdateObject (denied test present, eval-failed test absent)
- MoveObject (denied test present, eval-failed test absent)
- AddSceneParticipant (denied test present, eval-failed test absent)
- RemoveSceneParticipant (denied test present, eval-failed test absent)
- ListSceneParticipants (denied test present, eval-failed test absent)
- FindLocationByName (denied test present, eval-failed test absent)
- MoveCharacter (denied test present, eval-failed test absent)

The distinction between ErrPermissionDenied and ErrAccessEvaluationFailed is a critical semantic contract: callers (like WhoHandler) use errors.Is() to decide whether to count an error as a transient system failure or a policy denial. If checkAccess erroneously wraps an engine error as ErrPermissionDenied for any of these methods, WhoHandler would silently drop sessions from the who list without incrementing the error count or showing a warning.

At minimum, GetExit, CreateObject, MoveObject, MoveCharacter, and AddSceneParticipant should get eval-failed cases since they cover the different entity prefixes.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T23:39:39Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-12T23:39:39Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-sdno.14","title":"audit Logger: DB-fail-WAL-ok degraded path has no test","description":"internal/access/policy/audit/logger.go adds a new code path (line 1377-1386 in diff) where the primary DB sync write fails but the WAL fallback succeeds. This path logs a warning and increments failuresCounter with label 'db_failed_wal_ok'. There is no test covering this scenario.

The existing tests cover:
- DB and WAL both fail → TestAuditLogger_BothDBAndWALFail_EntryDropped
- DB succeeds (happy path)

Missing: a test where mockWriter.failSync=true but WAL path is a valid file. This should verify:
1. Log returns nil (not an error — entry is durable via WAL)
2. failuresCounter('db_failed_wal_ok') increments
3. slog.Warn 'audit DB write failed, fell back to WAL' is emitted
4. The entry is NOT written to the DB writer's syncWrites list

This path is critical for ops visibility into audit degradation. Without a test, the metric label string 'db_failed_wal_ok' could be silently changed, breaking alert routing.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T23:39:18Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-12T23:39:18Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-sdno.28","title":"GetCharactersByLocation silently denies all requests after action rename to list_characters","description":"internal/world/service.go — GetCharactersByLocation changed its access action from \"read\" to \"list_characters\" (line ~3460 in diff). Any seed policy that granted \"read\" on \"location:\u003cid\u003e\" for character enumeration will no longer match; the engine will default-deny every such request without logging a policy denial (it logs an infra failure or a policy denial depending on seed state). The change references ADR #76 but no seed policy update is visible in this diff. Until seed policies are updated to use action \"list_characters\", all GetCharactersByLocation calls will fail-closed and real characters will silently disappear from location views (e.g., look command). Fix: add the \"list_characters\" action to the default seed policies before or atomically with this PR landing.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-13T00:35:28Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-13T00:35:28Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-sdno.29","title":"ExamineLocation/Object/Character changed error codes from EXAMINE_ACCESS_DENIED without updating tests","description":"internal/world/service.go — ExamineLocation, ExamineObject, and ExamineCharacter previously returned EXAMINE_ACCESS_DENIED when access was denied. After this PR they return the checkAccess helper error which uses prefixLocation/Object/Character + _ACCESS_DENIED (e.g., LOCATION_ACCESS_DENIED for ExamineLocation). The test file (internal/world/service_test.go lines ~3941, 4168, 4413) still asserts errutil.AssertErrorCode(t, err, \"EXAMINE_ACCESS_DENIED\"). This means the test suite will fail after merge. Additionally, any caller outside this codebase that error-matches on EXAMINE_ACCESS_DENIED will silently stop handling those errors. Fix: update service_test.go to assert the new codes (LOCATION_ACCESS_DENIED, OBJECT_ACCESS_DENIED, CHARACTER_ACCESS_DENIED). Confirm no callers outside this PR match on EXAMINE_ACCESS_DENIED.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-13T00:35:35Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-13T00:35:35Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-sdno.31","title":"GetCharactersByLocation access denied returns LOCATION_ACCESS_DENIED, not CHARACTER_ACCESS_DENIED","description":"internal/world/service.go GetCharactersByLocation calls checkAccess with prefixLocation (not prefixCharacter). This means access denials on character-by-location queries emit error code LOCATION_ACCESS_DENIED instead of CHARACTER_ACCESS_DENIED. For runtime behavior this is harmless (callers use errors.Is sentinel checks, not code matching), but it means metrics/alerting on CHARACTER_ACCESS_DENIED will under-count character-access denials and LOCATION_ACCESS_DENIED will over-count. Consider using prefixCharacter or a dedicated prefix for this query, or document that this is intentional because the gating resource is the location.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-13T00:35:42Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-13T00:35:42Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-sdno.30","title":"[code] ModeMinimal audit behavior change silently drops system bypass events","description":"File: internal/access/policy/audit/logger.go, around line 464-466 in the diff.

ModeMinimal previously logged EffectSystemBypass events synchronously. The PR removes system bypass from the ModeMinimal case silently — the new behavior is described in the updated const comment and in shouldLog, but:

1. This is a breaking change for operators who rely on ModeMinimal to audit system bypass events for security monitoring.
2. There is no deprecation notice, migration note, or changelog entry.
3. The PR summary does not mention this behavioral change.
4. The updated audit_mode comment says ModeMinimal logs 'denials and default denials only', while ModeDenialsOnly now logs 'all denials, default denials, and system bypasses'. This means system bypass visibility requires upgrading from ModeMinimal to ModeDenialsOnly — a significant operational change.

Suggested fix: Add a clear comment in the CHANGELOG or PR description explaining why system bypasses were removed from ModeMinimal. Also consider whether existing deployments using ModeMinimal need a migration notice. If the intent is that system bypasses should always be logged, consider adding them back to ModeMinimal or adding a new explicit mode for 'denials + bypasses'.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-13T00:35:42Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-13T00:35:42Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-sdno.33","title":"boot.go findCharacterByName player-facing message embedded in oops.Errorf is silently discarded by PlayerMessage","description":"internal/command/handlers/boot.go findCharacterByName (line ~1966 in diff): the access evaluation failure path returns oops.Code(command.CodeAccessEvaluationFailed).Errorf(\"Unable to search...\"). However, PlayerMessage() matches CodeAccessEvaluationFailed and returns a hardcoded string \"Permission check failed. Please try again or contact an administrator.\" — the message embedded in Errorf is never shown to the player. This is not a security issue (the right message is still shown) but it creates maintenance confusion: future authors may think the Errorf message is what players see, and modify it without effect. {"id":"holomush-sdno.37","title":"no test for WhoHandler anomaly-detection log when all sessions denied (zero results, non-zero sessions)","description":"The production WhoHandler has an anomaly detection path that logs a warning when all sessions are visible (no engine errors, no DB errors) yet the output list is empty — indicating every character was explicitly denied read access. The test diff adds TestWhoHandler_AllAccessEvaluationFailedShowsNoPlayersWithError (engine errors) and TestWhoHandler_SkipsInaccessibleCharacters (one denied, one visible), but there is no test for the scenario: N sessions, N explicit-deny results, 0 engine errors, 0 DB errors — which would trigger the anomaly log. {"id":"holomush-sdno.38","title":"list_commands incomplete field not tested in Lua integration tests for partial circuit-breaker trip","description":"TestListCommands_CircuitBreakerTripsAfterThreeErrors in commands_test.go correctly verifies the 'incomplete' field on the result table when the circuit breaker trips (all 6 commands with capabilities, 3 engine errors). new_value: {"notes":"On re-read: the per-command circuit breaker counting is documented in the code comment as intentional design. Not a bug.","status":"closed"}
old_value:
{"id":"holomush-sdno.48","title":"canExecuteCommand returns (false, false) for infra-failure deny without setting hadError","description":"In internal/plugin/hostfunc/commands.go, canExecuteCommand (~line 13588-13596), when decision.IsAllowed() is false AND decision.IsInfraFailure() is false (normal policy deny), hadError remains false and the function returns (false, false). This is correct.

However, the order of operations reveals a subtle gap: when decision.IsInfraFailure() is true, hadError is set to true and then 'return false, hadError' is executed — this is correct.

But the real issue is in the CALLER: listCommandsFn accumulates 'hadEngineError' from canExecuteCommand's hadError return, so when a command is denied due to infra failure, the 'incomplete' flag IS set correctly.

The subtle bug is: canExecuteCommand returns early (return false, hadError) on the FIRST capability failure for a multi-capability command. If capability[0] is denied by infra failure (hadError=true) and capability[1] would have been allowed, the command is marked as errored and hidden. This is actually the correct fail-closed behavior, but the function comment says 'Returns (allowed bool, hadError bool) where hadError is true if ANY engine evaluation failed' — this is accurate.

The actual issue to flag: the circuit breaker in listCommandsFn counts engineErrorCount per *command*, but canExecuteCommand short-circuits on the FIRST failed capability (correct fail-closed). If a command has 5 capabilities and they all fail, it counts as only 1 engine error toward the circuit breaker threshold. This matches the documented invariant ('Each command contributes at most 1 to the count'), but it means a command with many capabilities won't trip the circuit breaker faster than a command with 1 capability even if it represents a sustained engine outage. The threshold of 3 may need to be re-evaluated given this per-command (not per-capability) counting.

This is a design nuance worth documenting in the circuit breaker threshold ADR rather than a code fix.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-13T01:11:02Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-13T01:11:02Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-sdno.51","title":"protect-main.sh hook deleted with no replacement guard","description":"The file .claude/hooks/protect-main.sh was deleted in this PR without any documented replacement. This hook was the Git-level guard preventing direct commits to main, complementing branch protection rules. Its removal means any workflow that invokes the hook silently loses that protection.

File removed: .claude/hooks/protect-main.sh

The PR diff shows the file as deleted but there is no added replacement hook, no updated lefthook.yaml reference, and no ADR or comment documenting why the protection is no longer needed. If the hook was superseded by server-side branch protection alone, that decision should be documented.

Suggested fix: Either restore the hook, replace it with an equivalent guard, or document in CLAUDE.md/commit why the protection was intentionally removed.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-13T01:14:14Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-13T01:14:14Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-sdno.49","title":"rate_limit_middleware: engine error swallowed — no error returned to Dispatch caller","description":"In internal/command/rate_limit_middleware.go Enforce() (~line 11962-11977), when hasBypass() returns an error, the code logs at Error level, records a metric, sets a span attribute, and then falls through to apply rate limiting (fail-closed). The error itself is intentionally NOT returned.

The comment explains the rationale: returning the error would block command execution entirely, which is worse than applying rate limits to a potentially exempt user. This reasoning is documented and defensible.

However, the behavior difference is not surfaced to the user at all — a rate-limited user whose bypass check failed will just see the rate limit message, with no indication that a system error occurred. This is intentional per the code comment but differs from other paths (who.go, commands.go) which do show '(Note: N players could not be displayed due to system errors)'.

Suggestion: This is a conscious tradeoff, but it's worth adding a note in the returned ErrRateLimited error or as a separate slog.WarnContext to the *user* output when the bypass check itself failed (vs. the user being legitimately rate-limited). The current code only tells *operators* via the error log; affected users get the same experience as genuinely rate-limited users.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-13T01:11:15Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-13T01:11:15Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-sdno.54","title":"Cache.Start() returns breaking error — callers not updated","description":"In internal/access/policy/cache.go, Cache.Start() was changed from a no-op that emitted a warning log to a function that returns an error with code CACHE_START_NOT_IMPLEMENTED. This is a breaking API change.

The concern is whether all callers of Start() — both in production wiring (cmd/holomush/deps.go or equivalent) and in any test setup — handle the new error return. The previous behavior was a silent no-op so callers may have been written to ignore the return value.

If any production call site does:
  _ = cache.Start()   // ignores error from old no-op
the new behavior now silently discards a meaningful error, potentially leaving the cache in a non-started state without alerting operators.

Suggested fix: Audit all call sites of Cache.Start() and Cache.StartWithListener() to confirm they handle the new error return. Add a test that verifies Start() errors if the cache has not been initialized with a listener.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-13T01:14:47Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-13T01:14:47Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-sdno.60","title":"Fix(holomush-sdno.52): Restore EffectSystemBypass to ModeMinimal audit","description":"Restore EffectSystemBypass to ModeMinimal shouldLog set in audit/logger.go","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-13T01:25:11Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-13T01:25:11Z"}

{"id":"holomush-sdno.52","title":"ModeMinimal audit silently drops EffectSystemBypass events","description":"In internal/access/policy/audit/logger.go, the set of effects logged under ModeMinimal was changed from {EffectDeny, EffectDefaultDeny, EffectSystemBypass} to {EffectDeny, EffectDefaultDeny}. EffectSystemBypass events are no longer logged in minimal mode.

This is a security audit regression: system bypasses (the highest-privilege path that skips all policy evaluation) are now invisible in minimal audit mode. Security reviews and incident investigations rely on bypass events being logged at all audit levels, as they represent uses of elevated privilege that should always be traceable.

ADR 0007 (command-security-model) and ADR 086 (audit-off-mode-logs-denials) both emphasize that security-sensitive events must be auditable. System bypass events are security-sensitive by definition.

The change appears in the shouldLog() method or equivalent filtering logic. No ADR or design document justifying the removal of SystemBypass from ModeMinimal was added.

Suggested fix: Restore EffectSystemBypass to the ModeMinimal log set, or document the intentional decision in an ADR with the security rationale.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-13T01:14:26Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-13T01:14:26Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-sdno.61","title":"Fix(holomush-sdno.50): Return informative Decision on attribute resolution error","description":"Return DefaultDeny Decision with reason instead of zero-value Decision{} when attribute resolution fails in engine.go","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-13T01:25:13Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-13T01:25:13Z"}

{"id":"holomush-sdno.50","title":"engine.go: attribute resolution error returns Decision{} (zero value) not an infra-failure Decision","description":"In internal/access/policy/engine.go Evaluate(), at the attribute resolution failure path (~line 2077):

  return types.Decision{}, oops.With(...).Wrap(resolveErr)

When attribute resolution fails, the engine returns a zero-value Decision{} and a non-nil error. Callers that receive a non-nil error are documented to treat it as denial (fail-closed), which is correct.

However, world/service.go checkAccess() and command/access.go CheckCapability() both check decision.IsInfraFailure() only when the error is nil (the non-nil error path takes precedence). So this is functionally correct.

The concern is subtler: checkAccess wraps the error with ErrAccessEvaluationFailed, but the zero-value Decision{} is discarded. This means callers cannot distinguish between 'engine returned an error and a zero Decision' vs 'engine returned an error and an infra-failure Decision'. Since the zero Decision has IsInfraFailure()=false (default), if any caller ever inspects the returned Decision alongside the error (not just the error), they would silently get the wrong infra-failure classification.

The engine should return a proper infra-failure decision alongside the error for attribute resolution failures, consistent with the session resolution failure path (~line 2008) which constructs types.NewDecision(types.EffectDefaultDeny, ...) before returning nil error. Or document that callers MUST NOT inspect the Decision when error != nil.

File: internal/access/policy/engine.go, attribute resolution failure block.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-13T01:11:28Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-13T01:11:28Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-sdno.47","title":"dispatcher.go: OTel span not marked as error on engine failure path","description":"In internal/command/dispatcher.go (the capability check loop, ~line 170-184), when CheckCapability returns a CodeAccessEvaluationFailed error (engine failure), the code correctly calls metrics.SetStatus(StatusEngineFailure) and returns the error, but it does NOT call span.RecordError(capErr) or span.SetStatus(codes.Error, ...) on the OTel span.

The rate_limit path correctly sets a span attribute (rate_limit.bypass_check_failed), and the span is used elsewhere in Dispatch, but the engine-failure capability-check path only updates the Prometheus metric — the OTel trace for that request will not be marked as an error, making it hard to correlate distributed traces with engine outages.

Suggested fix: before returning capErr in the engine failure branch, add:
  span.RecordError(capErr)
  span.SetStatus(codes.Error, "capability check engine failure")

This is the same pattern already used elsewhere in Dispatch for command execution errors.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-13T01:10:46Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-13T01:10:46Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-sdno.62","title":"Fix(holomush-sdno.47): Add OTel span error on engine failure in dispatcher","description":"Add span.RecordError and span.SetStatus on engine failure path in dispatcher.go capability check","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-13T01:25:14Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-13T01:25:14Z"}

{"id":"holomush-sdno.63","title":"Fix(holomush-sdno.55): Extract shared maxEngineErrors constant","description":"Extract maxEngineErrors=3 into a shared location used by both who.go and hostfunc/commands.go","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-13T01:33:45Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-13T01:33:45Z"}

{"id":"holomush-sdno.55","title":"maxEngineErrors circuit breaker threshold duplicated across who.go and commands.go","description":"The circuit breaker threshold maxEngineErrors = 3 is defined in two independent places:

1. internal/command/handlers/who.go: package-level const maxEngineErrors = 3
2. internal/plugin/hostfunc/commands.go: local const maxEngineErrors = 3

The code comments acknowledge this separation ('Independent from hostfunc/commands.go') but the values are duplicated without a shared constant. If the threshold needs to change in the future, both locations must be updated independently. Because they are in different packages with different purposes (session iteration vs. registry iteration) the duplication is defensible, but the lack of any shared definition or cross-reference makes the relationship implicit.

Suggested fix: Either (a) export a shared constant from the observability or access/policy package (e.g., observability.DefaultCircuitBreakerThreshold = 3) and reference it from both locations, or (b) add a comment in each file explicitly cross-referencing the other location so future changes are not accidentally applied to only one.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-13T01:14:59Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-13T01:14:59Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-sdno.64","title":"Fix(holomush-sdno.59): Add incomplete==false assertion to list_commands test","description":"Add assertion that incomplete==false on success path in list_commands test","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-13T02:09:16Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-13T02:09:16Z"}

{"id":"holomush-sdno.59","title":"list_commands unit test does not assert incomplete==false on the success path","description":"internal/plugin/hostfunc/commands_test.go adds TestListCommands_CircuitBreakerTripsAfterThreeErrors which verifies incomplete==true when errors exceed the threshold, but no unit test asserts that incomplete==false on the normal success path (zero engine errors).

The existing success-path tests (TestListCommands_AllowAll, TestListCommands_DenyAll, etc.) were updated to use the new {commands, incomplete} return table structure, but none explicitly asserts result['incomplete'] == false. If the field were accidentally initialized to true or changed to a truthy default, all existing success-path tests would still pass.

This is partially covered by the integration test in internal/plugin/help_integration_test.go, but unit tests should self-document the expected contract without requiring a full integration setup.

Suggested fix: In TestListCommands_AllowAll (or any single success-path test), add an assertion: assert.Equal(t, lua.LFalse, result.RawGetString('incomplete')).

File: internal/plugin/hostfunc/commands_test.go","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-13T01:15:54Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-13T01:15:54Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-sdno.66","title":"Fix(holomush-sdno.57): Add engine-error and infra-failure tests to boot_test","description":"Add test cases for engine error and infra-failure paths in boot capability check","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-13T02:09:16Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-13T02:09:16Z"}

{"id":"holomush-sdno.57","title":"boot_test.go missing engine-error and infra-failure test cases for capability checks","description":"internal/command/handlers/boot_test.go was updated to use MockAccessPolicyEngine but only exercises allow (grant present) and default-deny (no grant) paths. There are no test cases for:
1. Engine returning an error (NewErrorEngine) — boot command should return an error or fall back gracefully
2. Engine returning an infra-failure decision (NewInfraFailureEngine) — should be treated as evaluation failure

The boot command handler is a critical initialization path. If the access engine fails during boot, the server may enter an inconsistent state. Verifying that engine failures are propagated correctly (not silently swallowed) is important defensive coverage.

Suggested fix: Add two table rows to the existing boot handler test:
- 'engine error' using policytest.NewErrorEngine(errors.New('db unavailable')) — assert handler returns error containing ErrAccessEvaluationFailed
- 'infra failure' using policytest.NewInfraFailureEngine() — assert same

File: internal/command/handlers/boot_test.go","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-13T01:15:37Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-13T01:15:37Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-sdno.67","title":"Fix(holomush-sdno.56): Add engine-error/infra-failure tests for world service","description":"Add dedicated test cases for engine-error and infra-failure paths in world service tests","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-13T02:09:16Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-13T02:09:16Z"}

{"id":"holomush-sdno.56","title":"World service migrated call sites lack dedicated engine-error and infra-failure test cases","description":"The checkAccess() helper in internal/world/service_helpers_test.go comprehensively tests the error/infra-failure paths at the helper level (TestCheckAccess covers allow, default_deny, engineError, explicit deny, infra failure, context.Canceled). However, the individual world service method tests beyond GetLocation and DeleteLocation do not have their own NewErrorEngine or NewInfraFailureEngine test cases.

Affected methods: UpdateLocation, GetExit, CreateExit, UpdateExit, DeleteExit, GetObject, CreateObject, UpdateObject, DeleteObject, GetCharacter, CreateCharacter, UpdateCharacter, MoveCharacter, GetScene, CreateScene, UpdateScene, DeleteScene — approximately 20+ operations in internal/world/service_test.go that only exercise the allow (via policytest.NewGrantEngine) and default-deny (no grant) paths.

The risk: a future refactoring that moves access checking inline (bypassing checkAccess) would silently lose error/infra-failure coverage. The tests at the helper level catch logic bugs in checkAccess itself, but not integration bugs where a method fails to call checkAccess at all or calls it with wrong arguments.

Suggested fix: Add at minimum one representative test per domain group (location, exit, object, character, scene) that passes NewErrorEngine and NewInfraFailureEngine and verifies the method returns ErrAccessEvaluationFailed. This is low cost — a single table-driven test row per method is sufficient.

File: internal/world/service_test.go","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-13T01:15:29Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-13T01:15:29Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88"}

{"id":"holomush-sdno.58","title":"rate_limit_middleware_test.go missing InfraFailureEngine test case","description":"internal/command/rate_limit_middleware_test.go covers engine error (TestRateLimitMiddleware_Enforce_EngineError) and explicit deny (TestRateLimitMiddleware_Enforce_DenyDecision) but does not test the infra-failure decision path (policytest.NewInfraFailureEngine).

Infra failures (where Decision.IsInfraFailure() returns true) are semantically distinct from policy denials: they signal that the access control system is degraded, not that the policy explicitly denied access. The rate limit middleware should treat infra failures consistently — whether that means denying the request, allowing it through, or returning a specific error — and this behavior should be tested.

Without a dedicated test, a future change to the middleware's handling of IsInfraFailure() decisions could silently change the security posture under degraded conditions.

Suggested fix: Add TestRateLimitMiddleware_Enforce_InfraFailure using policytest.NewInfraFailureEngine() and verify that the middleware returns ErrAccessEvaluationFailed (not ErrPermissionDenied, and not silently allows through).

{"id":"holomush-ycrv.17","title":"guest name reuse integration test is unreliable — relies on random pool position after release","description":"The 'releases guest name after disconnect' integration test in test/integration/telnet/e2e_test.go is checking the wrong invariant. Rather than verifying that the active count decreases (which is correct and is also tested), the previous plan variant connected 5 guests, disconnected them, reconnected 5 more and expected a name collision. The actual merged test correctly uses Eventually on ActiveCount, which is reliable. However, the plan doc still contains the flawed 5+5 collision variant (lines 906-941 of the plan) which was not implemented — so the actual code is fine. No code change needed, but the plan doc contains misleading pseudo-code that could confuse future implementers.\\n\\nUpdated assessment: the actual e2e_test.go uses the reliable Eventually+ActiveCount pattern. This finding is downgraded to suggestion-level: consider removing the misleading 5+5 name-collision pseudocode from the plan doc.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:32:35Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-17T03:32:35Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/117"}

{"id":"holomush-ycrv.2","title":"arrive event emitted after session is stored, leaving a window where event fails silently with no session rollback","description":"In internal/grpc/server.go Authenticate, HandleConnect is called after sessionStore.Set succeeds. When the arrive-event append fails (e.g. DB unavailable), the error is logged at Warn and the response is still returned as a success. This is the intended best-effort design (documented in the spec and the inline comment).

However, the log entry uses slog.WarnContext rather than errutil.LogError, so the structured oops context (operation, wrapped cause) is not fully surfaced. The warn message 'arrive event failed' does not include the session_id, which would help correlate the warning to the session that was just created.

Location: internal/grpc/server.go ~line 1611-1618 in the diff.

Suggested improvement (not a correctness bug, but a logging quality issue):
  Add 'session_id', sessionID.String() to the WarnContext call so the event can be correlated to the session in log queries. Consider using errutil.LogError at Warn level for consistent oops unwrapping.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:31:39Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-17T03:31:39Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/117"}

{"id":"holomush-ycrv.5","title":"Integration test uses time.Sleep for eventual consistency instead of Eventually","description":"test/integration/telnet/e2e_test.go — The two lifecycle tests 'emits arrive event on guest connect' and 'emits leave event on guest disconnect' use time.Sleep(200 * time.Millisecond) to wait for event persistence, rather than the project-mandated Eventually pattern. This makes them inherently flaky under load or on slow CI runners, and violates the integration testing spec which requires Eventually for async operations (see CLAUDE.md testing section and the Ginkgo pattern used elsewhere in e2e_test.go). The 'releases guest name after disconnect' test correctly uses Eventually. Fix: replace the fixed 200ms sleep with Eventually(func() [...], 5*time.Second, 100*time.Millisecond) that polls until the event is present in the store.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:32:08Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-17T03:32:08Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/117"}
