old_value: {"id":"holomush-0c3r.6","title":"Config file path read twice for game section — TOCTOU race on resolveConfigPath","description":"In cmd/holomush/core.go, config.Load is called twice with the same configFile path — once in RunE (line 90) for the core section, and again inside runCoreWithDeps (line 348) for the game section. resolveConfigPath calls os.Stat() to check file existence before reading (a TOCTOU check-then-use pattern). Between the two calls, the file could be replaced (e.g., a symlink swap). While the risk in practice is low for a local config file, the double-load is architecturally unnecessary and creates two separate TOCTOU windows where a single load would eliminate the issue entirely. The same os.Stat pattern in resolveConfigPath (lines 84-88 and 100-103 of internal/config/config.go) stat's before opening — the underlying koanf file.Provider will os.Open separately, creating the check-then-use gap. Recommended fix: Read the config file once in RunE, pass the loaded GameConfig into runCoreWithDeps as a parameter, or load both sections in a single pass.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-18T03:50:40Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-18T03:50:40Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/118"}

{"id":"holomush-0c3r.5","title":"GameConfig loaded twice in core RunE — second Load call not tested for error","description":"core.go calls config.Load() twice: once in RunE for coreConfig (line 90), and again inside runCoreWithDeps for GameConfig (line 348). The second call uses the same configFile global but is buried inside the telnet/gRPC setup block. TestCoreCommand_ConfigFileLoading only covers the first Load call (coreConfig). There is no test that (a) verifies guest_start_location is read from the config file, (b) verifies the fallback ULID is used when game.guest_start_location is absent, or (c) verifies that a CONFIG_GAME_FAILED error is surfaced when the second Load call fails (e.g., malformed game section). The second Load path can fail silently in a regression because all existing tests bypass runCoreWithDeps' full execution path. File: cmd/holomush/core.go:343-352.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-18T03:50:35Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-18T03:50:35Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/118"}

{"id":"holomush-0c3r.12","title":"bug: config.Load called twice for the same file during core startup — once in RunE, once inside runCoreWithDeps","description":"In cmd/holomush/core.go, config.Load is called twice on every core startup:

1. Line ~90 in RunE: config.Load(configFile, cmd, cfg, "core") — loads coreConfig
2. Line ~348 in runCoreWithDeps: config.Load(configFile, cmd, &gameConfig, "game") — loads GameConfig

Both calls open and parse the same YAML file (or both use the same XDG default path resolution). The file is read, parsed, and fully unmarshaled twice. While this is not functionally incorrect and the spec explicitly acknowledges it ('The double file read is acceptable for a startup-only operation'), it is wasteful and slightly surprising.

More importantly, the second call at line 348 uses the package-level configFile variable directly (captured via closure from root.go's var), not through any function parameter. This creates an implicit dependency on a package-level global inside what is otherwise a testable function that takes explicit dependencies. Tests that call runCoreWithDeps directly bypass both Load calls, so the game config loading path (configurable guest start location) is untested in the unit test suite.

The plan acknowledged this and called it acceptable. However, the GameConfig load inside runCoreWithDeps means the configurable guest_start_location feature is not exercised by any unit test in the PR — the integration path is only reachable when the full command runs.

Suggested fix: Either move the GameConfig loading into RunE alongside the coreConfig loading (passing startLocationID as a parameter to runCoreWithDeps), or accept the current design but add a unit test that exercises the guest start location path.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-18T03:51:04Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-18T03:51:04Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/118"}

{"id":"holomush-0c3r.4","title":"resolveConfigPath uses string concatenation instead of filepath.Join","description":"In internal/config/config.go line 99, the default config path is constructed with string concatenation:

  defaultPath := configDir + "/config.yaml"

The import for 'path/filepath' is not present in this file. Using filepath.Join is the idiomatic Go approach and avoids potential issues with trailing slashes in configDir:

  defaultPath := filepath.Join(configDir, "config.yaml")

This is a minor consistency issue — the xdg.ConfigDir() implementation likely returns a clean path without trailing slash, so there is no functional bug today, but filepath.Join is the established convention for path construction in Go.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-18T03:50:34Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-18T03:50:34Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/118"}

{"id":"holomush-0c3r.22","title":"Documentation precedence list incorrectly places env vars as layer 2 above config file","description":"site/docs/operators/configuration.md lines 9-12 list the precedence hierarchy as: 1. CLI flags, 2. Environment variables, 3. Config file, 4. Defaults. The spec's Design Decisions section defines a two-layer model (config file \u003c CLI flags) and explicitly states 'No environment variable layer for config keys. DATABASE_URL remains the sole env var, handled separately.' DATABASE_URL is not part of the config loading stack at all — it is a separate os.Getenv call. Listing it as layer 2 in the precedence hierarchy misrepresents the architecture and could confuse operators who might expect e.g. HOLOMUSH_GRPC_ADDR env vars to work. The precedence table in the 'Precedence Rules' subsection (around line 287) is accurate. The Overview list at the top should be corrected to reflect the actual two-layer model, with a note that DATABASE_URL is env-only and outside this stack.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-18T03:51:54Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-18T03:51:54Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/118"}

{"id":"holomush-0c3r.24","title":"Fix(holomush-0c3r.19): address review finding","description":"Fix for holomush-0c3r.19","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-18T04:09:50Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-18T04:09:50Z"}

{"id":"holomush-0c3r.19","title":"Documentation lists --guest-start-location flag that does not exist","description":"The operator documentation at site/docs/operators/configuration.md line 383 documents '# Flag: --guest-start-location' for the game.guest_start_location config key. No such CLI flag exists — the spec explicitly states 'no --guest-start-location flag exists' and the core command only registers grpc-addr, control-addr, metrics-addr, data-dir, game-id, log-format, and skip-seed-migrations flags. This creates operator confusion: an operator following the docs would try 'holomush core --guest-start-location=...' and get an error. The comment should either be removed or replaced with '# No CLI flag equivalent; config file or default seed ULID only'.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-18T03:51:38Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-18T03:51:38Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/118"}

{"id":"holomush-0c3r.10","title":"docs: configuration.md precedence list misordered — env vars listed above config file but that's not accurate","description":"site/docs/operators/configuration.md lines 7-11 lists the precedence hierarchy as:
1. Command-line flags (highest precedence)
2. Environment variables (DATABASE_URL is env-only — it has no config file equivalent)
3. Config file (~/.config/holomush/config.yaml)
4. Built-in defaults

This is misleading and inaccurate. The spec and implementation define a two-layer system (config file < CLI flags). DATABASE_URL has no config file equivalent so it does not belong in this precedence stack at all — it is orthogonal. Listing it as layer 2 implies that other environment variables could sit between CLI flags and the config file, which is not true. An operator reading this could incorrectly expect that env vars override the config file for settings other than DATABASE_URL.

Fix: Remove environment variables from the ordered precedence list, and mention DATABASE_URL as a side note explaining it is orthogonal (env-only, not part of the layered system). For example:
1. Command-line flags (highest precedence)
2. Config file (~/.config/holomush/config.yaml)
3. Built-in defaults

Note: DATABASE_URL is env-only and does not participate in this hierarchy.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-18T03:50:51Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-18T03:50:51Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/118"}

{"id":"holomush-0c3r.27","title":"Fix(holomush-0c3r.18): address review finding","description":"Fix for holomush-0c3r.18","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-18T04:09:51Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-18T04:09:51Z"}

{"id":"holomush-0c3r.14","title":"bug: resolveConfigPath uses string concatenation instead of filepath.Join for XDG default path","description":"In internal/config/config.go, resolveConfigPath constructs the default path using string concatenation:

    defaultPath := configDir + "/config.yaml"

This should use filepath.Join for portability and correctness:

    defaultPath := filepath.Join(configDir, "config.yaml")

While this is unlikely to cause problems on Linux/macOS (the primary targets), it is inconsistent with the rest of the codebase and violates the standard Go idiom. If configDir ever has a trailing slash (which xdg.ConfigDir() could return on some platforms or in tests), the concatenation would produce a double-slash. filepath.Join handles this correctly.

File: internal/config/config.go, line ~90 of the resolveConfigPath function.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-18T03:51:11Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-18T03:51:11Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/118"}

{"id":"holomush-0c3r.18","title":"Silent XDG failure swallows config entirely with no log or warning","description":"File: internal/config/config.go, lines 164-167.

When xdg.ConfigDir() returns an error (no HOME, broken environment), the code does:
  return "", false, nil //nolint:nilerr // intentional: missing XDG dir is not a config error

The intent is documented and reasonable for truly missing HOME. However, there is no log.Warn or slog.Debug call here. If this triggers due to a misconfigured environment on a production server, the operator sees no indication that config file loading was skipped — the process starts silently with only CLI defaults and no config file applied.

This is a silent degradation that could be very confusing in production (e.g., container launched with an unusual UID that has no /etc/passwd entry).

Fix: emit a debug or warn log before returning nil:
  slog.Warn("XDG config dir unavailable, skipping config file", "error", err)
  return "", false, nil

Alternatively use slog.Debug if this is considered too noisy for normal container runs.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-18T03:51:36Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-18T03:51:36Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/118"}

{"id":"holomush-0c3r.11","title":"No file permission check on loaded config file — world-readable secrets risk","description":"internal/config/config.go loads YAML from any file path without checking its permissions. The test files are written with 0o644 (world-readable). The operator documentation (site/docs/operators/configuration.md) does not recommend or require restrictive permissions. While DATABASE_URL is intentionally excluded from config files, future operators may be tempted to add sensitive values (TLS key paths, game secrets) to the config. The config package should either: (1) warn or error if the config file is world-readable (mode \u0026 0o044 != 0), similar to how SSH handles known_hosts, or (2) document explicitly in the operator docs that the config file should be chmod 0o600 and owned by the service user. The test in config_test.go TestLoad_FromYAMLFile uses 0o644 which would pass even if a permission check were added, since it runs as the file owner. The production recommendation should be 0o600.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-18T03:50:58Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-18T03:50:58Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/118"}

{"id":"holomush-0c3r.13","title":"ULID from config file is parsed without format validation beyond ulid.Parse","description":"In cmd/holomush/core.go lines 201-207, the guest_start_location value read from config (or the hardcoded fallback) is parsed via ulid.Parse(startLocationStr). If the config file contains a crafted value that is a valid ULID but corresponds to a non-existent or malicious location in the database, the server starts without error. This is a low-severity concern because: (1) the config file is operator-controlled, (2) ulid.Parse provides format validation, and (3) the location validity is checked downstream when guests actually connect. However, there is no startup-time validation that the configured ULID refers to a real location in the world — the error surfaces only when a guest connects. Consider adding a startup probe that verifies the configured start location exists, or at minimum document that an invalid location ID causes guests to fail authentication rather than preventing server start.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-18T03:51:07Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-18T03:51:07Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/118"}

{"id":"holomush-0c3r.16","title":"task: TestLoad_DefaultPathMissing_NoError asserts cobra defaults after Load but cfg was never wired to flags","description":"In internal/config/config_test.go, TestLoad_DefaultPathMissing_NoError does:

    cfg := &testConfig{}
    cmd := newTestCmd(cfg)

    err := Load("", cmd, cfg, "server")
    require.NoError(t, err)
    assert.Equal(t, "localhost:8080", cfg.Addr)

The test asserts cfg.Addr == "localhost:8080" (the cobra flag default). However, Load does not copy cobra flag defaults into the struct — the posflag provider only copies *explicitly-set* flags. When no file is loaded and no flags are set, cfg.Addr remains the zero value ("") unless cobra's StringVar already wrote the default into the struct pointer.

This works here only because newTestCmd calls cmd.Flags().StringVar(&cfg.Addr, "addr", "localhost:8080", ...) which writes the default directly into cfg.Addr at flag registration time. So the test passes not because Load sets the value, but because cobra already set it before Load was called.

The comment // Should have cobra defaults since no file exists is subtly misleading — the defaults come from cobra's flag registration, not from Load. This is fine behavior but the test comment should be clarified to avoid confusion about what Load actually provides when nothing is configured.

This is a documentation/clarity issue, not a functionality bug.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-18T03:51:21Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-18T03:51:21Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/118"}

{"id":"holomush-0c3r.17","title":"CONFIG_GAME_FAILED wraps already-rich error without adding context, making it a noisy no-op","description":"File: cmd/holomush/core.go, around line 349.

The code wraps the Load error:
  return oops.Code("CONFIG_GAME_FAILED").Wrap(loadErr)

config.Load already returns an oops error carrying a specific code (CONFIG_LOAD_FAILED, CONFIG_PARSE_FAILED, CONFIG_NOT_FOUND, CONFIG_UNMARSHAL_FAILED) plus a 'path' context field. The outer .Code("CONFIG_GAME_FAILED") overrides the inner code without adding any new fields like 'operation' or 'section', so it destroys information (the specific inner code) without adding any.

Either:
a) Drop the outer wrap: return loadErr  — the inner error is already actionable.
b) If the outer code is needed for callers to distinguish game-config failures from other config failures, at minimum add an operation field:
   return oops.Code("CONFIG_GAME_FAILED").With("operation", "load game config").Wrap(loadErr)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-18T03:51:27Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-18T03:51:27Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/118"}

{"id":"holomush-0c3r.21","title":"Malformed YAML on explicit --config path returns CONFIG_LOAD_FAILED not CONFIG_PARSE_FAILED","description":"The spec (Testing Strategy section) states: 'Malformed YAML → error with CONFIG_PARSE_FAILED code and file path context'. The implementation in internal/config/config.go lines 50-53 returns CONFIG_LOAD_FAILED (not CONFIG_PARSE_FAILED) when an explicitly-set --config path contains malformed YAML. CONFIG_PARSE_FAILED is only returned for the default XDG path. The spec does not differentiate error codes by whether the path was explicit or default — it specifies CONFIG_PARSE_FAILED for all malformed YAML. Additionally, the test in config_test.go TestLoad_MalformedYAML_ReturnsError does not assert the error code, so this deviation goes undetected. Fix: use CONFIG_PARSE_FAILED for both explicit and default paths, or update the spec to document the distinction and add a test asserting the explicit-path error code.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-18T03:51:46Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-18T03:51:46Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/118"}

{"id":"holomush-0c3r.9","title":"Config error messages leak full filesystem paths to operator CLI output","description":"In internal/config/config.go, error messages returned to the caller include the full filesystem path in two places: (1) resolveConfigPath line 87 — 'config file not found: %s' with configPath verbatim, and (2) the oops context With('path', configPath) and With('path', defaultPath) throughout. For a server-side daemon run by operators, leaking paths in error output to stderr/logs is generally acceptable and the risk is LOW. However, for the status subcommand (a client-side CLI tool), the path is also exposed. Since all config paths come from the operator themselves (via --config or XDG env), not from user input, the disclosure risk is limited to the operator's own environment. No immediate fix required, but the pattern is worth noting if the config loader is ever reused in a context where paths originate from untrusted input.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-18T03:50:49Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-18T03:50:49Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/118"}

{"id":"holomush-0c3r.1","title":"No test for config file error code propagation (CONFIG_NOT_FOUND vs CONFIG_LOAD_FAILED)","description":"config_test.go TestLoad_ExplicitPathMissing_ReturnsError asserts the path appears in the error message, but does not assert the oops error code is CONFIG_NOT_FOUND. Separately, there is no test that covers an explicit path that exists but is unreadable (permission denied) — which hits the CONFIG_LOAD_FAILED branch rather than CONFIG_NOT_FOUND. These two error codes are the primary actionable signals operators would act on, and a regression that swaps them would pass the existing test. File: internal/config/config.go:84-91 (resolveConfigPath). Suggested fix: add errutil.AssertErrorCode(t, err, \"CONFIG_NOT_FOUND\") to TestLoad_ExplicitPathMissing_ReturnsError, and add a test that chmod 000s the file to cover CONFIG_LOAD_FAILED.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-18T03:50:13Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-18T03:50:13Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/118"}

{"id":"holomush-0c3r.8","title":"config_test.go: default XDG path with non-ErrNotExist stat error has no test","description":"resolveConfigPath (internal/config/config.go:100-103) has three branches for the default XDG path: (1) file not found — silent, (2) other stat error (e.g., EPERM on directory) — returns CONFIG_ACCESS_FAILED, (3) file exists — load it. TestLoad_DefaultPathMissing_NoError covers branch 1 and TestLoad_DefaultXDGPath covers branch 3. Branch 2 (CONFIG_ACCESS_FAILED) is untested. While hard to trigger in normal circumstances, it is a production-observable failure mode when directory permissions change and would be invisible in test. A test can simulate this on Linux with a stat-failing path by making the parent directory non-executable, or by using a path inside a file rather than a directory.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-18T03:50:43Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-18T03:50:43Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/118"}

{"id":"holomush-0c3r.23","title":"Fix(holomush-0c3r.3): address review finding","description":"Fix for holomush-0c3r.3","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-18T04:09:50Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-18T04:09:50Z"}

{"id":"holomush-0c3r.2","title":"Boolean flag (--json) conflict with config file json: true not tested","description":"statusConfig.JSONOutput maps to koanf tag 'json', and the flag is named '--json'. The flag-name-to-koanf-key normalization in Load() does: section + '.' + strings.ReplaceAll(f.Name, '-', '_'). For '--json' this produces 'status.json', which matches the koanf tag. The TestStatusCommand_ConfigFileLoading test does not register the --json flag on its throwaway cobra.Command, so it never exercises the precedence path. More critically, no test covers the case where the config file sets json: true but the user passes --json=false (explicit false), nor where --json is omitted and the config file sets json: true. A bug in the posflag ProviderWithFlag callback (which uses Changed to detect explicit flags) could silently ignore the config value without any test catching it. File: cmd/holomush/status_test.go:1275-1291 and internal/config/config.go:62-68. Suggested fix: add a table-driven test in config_test.go with a bool field covering (1) config=true, flag not set → true; (2) config=true, flag explicitly false → false; (3) config=false, flag explicitly true → true.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-18T03:50:24Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-18T03:50:24Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/118"}

old_value: {"id":"holomush-qwom.4","title":"Spec lifecycle step 1 (assign connection ID) not implemented in GatewayHandler","description":"The spec's Connection Lifecycle (docs/superpowers/specs/2026-03-16-telnet-e2e-vertical-slice-design.md) step 1 says: 'Accept TCP connection, assign connection ID (ULID)'. The GatewayHandler implementation in internal/telnet/gateway_handler.go does not generate or track a per-connection ULID. This is a minor omission since the session ID from gRPC auth serves as the session identifier, but the spec explicitly calls for a connection-level ULID. This could matter for logging and debugging connection issues before auth completes.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-17T00:42:31Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-17T00:42:31Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/116"}

old_value:
{"id":"holomush-qwom.10","title":"No connection rate limiting on telnet accept loop","description":"File: cmd/holomush/gateway.go — runTelnetAcceptLoop

The telnet accept loop (and the GatewayHandler it spawns) has no per-IP or global connection rate limiting. An attacker can open connections faster than the name pool is exhausted (finding holomush-qwom.1), or simply exhaust OS-level socket resources and goroutine memory.

Each accepted connection spawns two goroutines (reader goroutine + event stream goroutine after auth), plus a bufio.Scanner buffer. With no limit, an attacker flooding TCP connections can cause OOM or goroutine exhaustion before the 400-name pool is even relevant.

Suggestion: Add a semaphore or token bucket in runTelnetAcceptLoop to cap concurrent connections (e.g. 200 max). This is a standard defense-in-depth measure for any public-facing listener. Alternatively enforce this at the network layer (firewall/load balancer), but document that expectation.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"4678+seanb4t@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-17T00:42:50Z","created_by":"seanb4t","updated_at":"2026-03-17T00:42:50Z","external_ref":"https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/116"}

No redundancy — active map is the only data structure. There is no pool slice in the current implementation.

All findings addressed — fixed, deferred, or closed as already resolved

old_value: {"id":"holomush-qwom.20","title":"Assertion strategy uses time.Sleep instead of spec-mandated Eventually() for async delivery","description":"The spec's Assertion Strategy section states: 'Use Eventually() with 5-second timeout for async event delivery'. The E2E test in test/integration/telnet/e2e_test.go uses time.Sleep(200 * time.Millisecond) in multiple BeforeEach blocks to wait for subscriptions and disconnect propagation (lines ~3551, 3597, 3641, 3661). While ReadUntil() provides retry-loop behavior, the BeforeEach sleep is a fixed delay rather than an Eventually() poll. If the system is slow (e.g., CI under load), 200ms may be insufficient. new_value: Root cause: telnet E2E waitForPipeline expected removed inline echo. Fixed to use broadcast event pattern.

{"description":"## Problem

The gRPC Subscribe handler uses an in-memory Broadcaster for live event delivery. This creates a race condition: the broadcaster subscription setup happens inside the server-streaming handler goroutine, but the gRPC client receives the stream object before the handler finishes setup. If HandleCommand fires before Subscribe's handler has called broadcaster.Subscribe(), the Broadcast call drops the event (broadcaster's default: branch for missing subscribers).

This manifests as: session persistence integration test hangs at stream.Recv() for the full context timeout — the say event was broadcast before the subscription existed.

## Root Cause

Two separate event delivery paths:
1. Engine calls eventStore.Append (Postgres INSERT + pg_notify) — persisted, durable
2. Engine calls broadcaster.Broadcast (in-memory channels) — ephemeral, races with setup

The gRPC Subscribe handler reads from path 2 (broadcaster) for live events, and path 1 (eventStore.Replay) for historical events.

## Fix

Replace the in-memory broadcaster with eventStore.Subscribe (Postgres LISTEN/NOTIFY) in the gRPC Subscribe handler for live events:

1. Subscribe handler calls eventStore.Subscribe(stream) for each stream — creates LISTEN connection, returns event ID channel
2. When event ID arrives via LISTEN, read the full event from store via Replay
3. Forward to gRPC stream

No race because: LISTEN is set up synchronously before handler returns. Append persists THEN notifies. LISTEN always receives notifications sent after setup.

## Files involved

- internal/grpc/server.go — Subscribe handler: replace broadcaster.Subscribe with eventStore.Subscribe, update forwardLiveEventsWithLocationFollow to read from event store
- internal/grpc/location_follow.go — locationFollower: replace broadcaster field with event store, update switchLocationSubscription
- internal/core/broadcaster.go — may become unused by Subscribe path (keep for other uses or remove)
- internal/core/store.go — MemoryEventStore.Subscribe is a stub that never delivers (line 98-107). Either make it work or remove MemoryEventStore
- internal/store/postgres.go — PostgresEventStore.Subscribe already works via LISTEN/NOTIFY

## MemoryEventStore

MemoryEventStore.Subscribe returns channels that NEVER emit events. This masks real behavior in unit tests. With testcontainers available, consider removing MemoryEventStore entirely — one event store, one subscription path, same behavior in tests and production.

## Connection concern

Each eventStore.Subscribe creates a dedicated Postgres connection. With 2 streams per session (location + character), that's 2 connections per player. For production scale, implement a shared LISTEN multiplexer that uses a single connection and fans out to subscribers. This is a follow-up optimization.

## Blocked by

PR #126 (command-response-events) depends on this fix — the integration test hangs without it.

## Key code references

- broadcaster race: internal/grpc/server.go lines 512-543 (subscription setup) vs engine.go lines 76-83 (HandleSay broadcast)
- event store Subscribe: internal/store/postgres.go line 240 — uses pg_notify/LISTEN, returns <-chan ulid.ULID
- MemoryEventStore stub: internal/core/store.go line 98-107 — Subscribe returns dead channels
- broadcaster drop: internal/core/broadcaster.go line 57 — default: branch drops events silently"}

This creates a race: the broadcaster subscription may not be set up when HandleCommand broadcasts, losing events. The fix: Subscribe handler should use eventStore.Subscribe (Postgres LISTEN) for live events. Events are persisted before NOTIFY fires — no race. new_value: Two root causes fixed: (1) core gRPC bound to localhost, unreachable from gateway container — added --grpc-addr 0.0.0.0:9000 to compose.yaml, (2) web client quit command didn't call disconnect() — intercepted quit in sendCommand. All 10 E2E tests pass.
